119.items: Anti-venom+ or antidote; Saradomin brews; Super restores; High-tier Magic gear (e.g. Trident of the swamp / Ahrim's or Ancestral, occult necklace)

120.stats: 40+ Attack/Strength/Defence or 40+ Ranged with 43+ Prayer; ~50 combat
120.setup: Any combat style. Melee with rune/addy weapon, or Ranged/Magic from a safespot. Greater demons have 0 melee defence and weakness to water/magic.
120.strategy: Plain kill count. Easiest F2P spot is the Greater demons in the Wilderness near Demonic Ruins or members' Brimhaven/Catacombs of Kourend; max hit is only 9 so even low-level players can tank it. No Slayer level required.

121.stats: 40+ Attack/Strength (Silverlight requires no stats), 43+ Prayer optional; ~55 combat
121.setup: Equip any demonbane weapon for the kill. Silverlight (F2P, from Demon Slayer quest) is the simplest; members can use Darklight/Arclight/Emberlight, Burning claws, Scorching bow, or cast demonbane spells.
121.strategy: You only need the FINAL hit to land with a demonbane weapon, so you can damage it with anything then swap to e.g. Silverlight to land the kill. Silverlight is the cheapest route for F2P; Darklight (Shadow of the Storm) or Arclight (Catacombs upgrade) for members.
121.items: Silverlight; Darklight; Arclight; Emberlight; Burning claws; Scorching bow; Purging staff; Holy water; Demonbane spells (e.g. Inferior/Superior/Dark Demonbane)

122.stats: 60+ Attack/Strength/Defence, or 70+ Magic for water spells, 43+ Prayer
122.setup: Any decent melee weapon (e.g. Dragon scimitar / abyssal whip) or water spells (Protect from Melee on); demonbane weapons also boost damage. Slayer task or Catacombs of Kourend.
122.strategy: Just land one kill. Hellhounds use melee only, so Protect from Melee fully blocks their damage; they have near-zero defence so almost any combat style works. Easiest as a Slayer assignment or in the Catacombs of Kourend.

123.setup: Slash melee (dragon scimitar / abyssal whip / scimitar) or Fire spells (slash is preferred for low slash defence; fire spells have 100% weakness bonus for lower levels).
123.strategy: Plant the Hespori seed, fight it 5 times total. Kill the 4 flower buds (10 HP each, one hit) at fight start, 66% and 33% HP, then DPS the boss; use Protect from Magic to cut its magic hit to 3.
123.items: Hespori seed; Rake; Seed dibber; Spade

124.setup: Any weapon with a special attack (dragon dagger spec, granite maul, voidwaker, etc.); bring full spec energy to the final phase.
124.strategy: Bring Hespori low (under ~10% HP after the last flower phase), then deliver the finishing hit with a special attack so the spec is what kills it. A granite maul or dragon dagger spec works well for the timing.
124.items: Dragon dagger; Dragon warhammer; Granite maul; Abyssal whip; Saradomin godsword; Voidwaker

125.setup: Stacked AoE: chinchompas (each bud has 10 HP and dies in one hit), or a fast multi-target ranged setup, to pop all four buds in one or two attacks.
125.strategy: The 4 flower buds each have 10 HP and die to a single hit. Use chinchompas (multi-target) to destroy all four in one throw, or rapidly target each individually; chins are the reliable way to hit the 5-second window.

126.stats: 65 Farming, 80+ Attack/Strength, high HP, strong food
126.setup: High DPS slash melee or fire spells with maximum offensive gear; bring plenty of food (sharks/brews) since you cannot use Protect from Magic/Missiles or any prayer.
126.strategy: Run with zero prayer active - turn off Quick-prayers and overheads. Eat through all damage instead of praying. Kill the buds fast and burst the boss to minimise the damage you must tank without protection prayers.

127.stats: 65 Farming, 90+ Ranged or 90+ Attack/Strength, 77+ Magic, 70+ Defence
127.setup: Best-in-slot DPS: twisted bow or strong slash melee; chinchompas to instantly clear flower phases; special-attack weapon (voidwaker/dragon dagger) to burst the boss.
127.strategy: Clear each flower phase in a single hit (chins) so you lose no DPS waiting, then maximise damage on the boss with spec weapons and best gear. Fire spells get a 100% weakness bonus if going magic; slash is best for melee.

128.stats: 65 Farming, 95+ Ranged/Attack/Strength, 77+ Magic, 75+ Defence, max gear
128.setup: Max DPS setup: twisted bow or top-tier slash weapon, chinchompas for instant flower clears, and multiple special-attack weapons (voidwaker, dragon dagger) for burst phases.
128.strategy: Very tight DPS check - instantly pop all 4 buds each phase with chins, then unload best-in-slot DPS plus spec weapons on the boss between phases. Pre-load full spec energy and use a high-damage style (tbow or maxed slash).
128.items: Twisted bow; Voidwaker; Dragon dagger; Chinchompa

129.setup: Rune scimitar + Protect from Missiles (F2P melee), or magic with earth spells; food and Prayer potions
129.strategy: Obtain a giant key from a Hill Giant kill, enter his lair below Edgeville dungeon, kill him once and loot the large spiked chest. Keep Protect from Missiles up to halve his ranged damage.

130.setup: Rune scimitar / dragon scimitar / abyssal whip with Protect from Missiles; bank trips between giant keys
130.strategy: Repeat the kill 5 times; you need a fresh giant key each kill, farmed from Hill Giants. Loot the chest each time to bank progress toward 5.

131.stats: 50+ Magic for Snare (members) or 20+ Magic for Bind (F2P); 60+ Ranged or strong melee for the kill
131.setup: Binding spell to immobilize (Snare/Entangle on members, Bind on F2P) plus ranged or magic DPS to finish him while frozen
131.strategy: Bind/Snare him and deal the final hit while the immobilize timer is still active. Recast the binding spell whenever it wears off so the killing blow lands while he is rooted. F2P must use Bind; members can use Snare/Entangle.
131.items: Giant key; Earth runes; Mind/chaos/death runes (binding spell)

132.setup: Position with your back against a wall so the knockback cannot push you more than one tile
132.strategy: Stand with a wall directly behind you (relative to Obor) before engaging, so when his slam knockback triggers you cannot be displaced more than one square. Kill him from that wall-locked position.

133.stats: 50+ Att/Str/Def, 43+ Prayer, decent HP; or ranged/magic equivalents
133.setup: Protect from Missiles kept on continuously (his ranged is his main threat and is halved by it); Prayer potions to keep it up the whole fight
133.strategy: Keep Protect from Missiles active for the entire fight and never let it drop. Any damage taken with prayer off fails the task, so flick or maintain the overhead and bring Prayer potions to outlast the kill.

134.setup: F2P gear: rune scimitar, rune armour, Protect from Missiles; food
134.strategy: Log into a free-to-play world and kill Obor there. Use F2P-only gear (rune scimitar) and Protect from Missiles to halve his ranged damage. Obor is already F2P-accessible so no membership-only items are needed.

135.setup: Ranged: Blowpipe (dragon darts) or DHCB, Masori/Armadyl or Crystal armour, Zaryte vambraces, anguish; heat-protective boots (boots of stone/brimstone/granite) unless elite Kourend diary done.
135.strategy: Plain kill count (75). Lure to the matching-colour vent each phase to keep its 75% damage reduction off; pray Protect from Missiles/Magic to match its style and chip away over multiple trips.
135.items: Toxic blowpipe; Dragon hunter crossbow; Twisted bow; Boots of stone

136.setup: Same ranged setup as KC tasks: Blowpipe or DHCB, Masori/Crystal, Zaryte vambraces, anguish, heat-protective boots; bring prayer pots/super restores and brews for long trips.
136.strategy: Plain kill count (150) - just a longer grind than the 75 KC. Use a Slayer task or efficient resets and run laps of the dungeon to keep killing.
136.items: Toxic blowpipe; Dragon hunter crossbow; Twisted bow; Boots of stone

137.setup: Standard ranged setup; nothing extra needed - this is a movement task.
137.strategy: The poison attack appears in the green phase: a 3x3 splatter blob plus ground pools (~12 dmg/tick). Run north on the Hydra's 4th attack to dodge the spawned blob and never stand in a pool.

138.setup: Standard ranged setup; movement task only.
138.strategy: Blue phase spawns a central electric ball that sends lightning from the four corners to your tile (binds + up to 22 dmg). Run west along the north wall, or trap it from the NW corner stepping one tile SE when the lightning is two tiles away.

139.setup: Standard ranged setup; movement task only.
139.strategy: Red phase walks to the centre, stuns you, then breathes a 5x5 flame and tracks fire (~25 dmg + burn). Turn run OFF and walk in a straight line away from the trailing fire so it never catches you.

140.strategy: An incorrect vent empowers the Hydra (up to +50% damage). Match each phase to its vent: green->red vent, blue->green vent, red->blue vent (grey final phase needs no vent). Position so the Hydra is only ever lured onto the correct colour.

141.setup: High-DPS ranged: Twisted bow or DHCB with best-in-slot gear and damage specs (e.g. Voidwaker/DDS) ready for the red phase.
141.strategy: A DPS race: enter the red phase with full special energy and burst it down before the Hydra walks to the centre to start the flame wall. The wiki recommends using damaging special attacks during the flame phase to phase it first.
141.items: Toxic blowpipe; Dragon hunter crossbow; Twisted bow; Boots of stone

142.setup: Standard ranged setup; flawless-execution task - master Protect prayer switches and all three special-attack dodges.
142.strategy: Combines all three avoidance tasks plus correct overhead prayers. Flick Protect from Missiles/Magic in sync with its 3-attack style switches (every attack in the final phase) and dodge acid, lightning and flame perfectly.

143.stats: Full Dharok's set, 70+ Strength/Attack, 74+ Prayer, high Hitpoints (to reduce to <=10 safely)
143.setup: Full Dharok's barrows set with the greataxe; rings/amulet for damage; bring the means to drop to <=10 HP (e.g. controlled damage) and stay there for the Dharok's set effect.
143.strategy: Dharok's set effect maxes damage at low HP. Reduce to 1-10 HP, then dodge every special perfectly because almost any hit kills you. Pray correctly and lure to correct vents; high-risk - the lowest completion rate (4.9%) of the set.
143.items: Dharok's greataxe; Dharok's helm; Dharok's platebody; Dharok's platelegs; Boots of stone

144.setup: Max ranged DPS: Twisted bow or DHCB with full Masori/best-in-slot, Lightbearer, divine ranging potion, full special energy and spec weapons.
144.strategy: Speed task. Always remove the 75% damage reduction via the correct vent at each phase start, maintain perfect prayer/DPS uptime, and spec at phase transitions to keep the kill under 1:45.
144.items: Twisted bow; Dragon hunter crossbow; Toxic blowpipe; Boots of stone

145.setup: Best-in-slot ranged only: Twisted bow / DHCB, full Masori (f), Zaryte vambraces, anguish, Lightbearer, divine ranging potion; Voidwaker/other specs for burst.
145.strategy: A very tight speedrun (GM, 3.6% completion). Requires near-perfect vent management, prayer flicking and spec timing - waste no ticks luring or repositioning; remove damage reduction instantly and burst each phase.
145.items: Twisted bow; Dragon hunter crossbow; Toxic blowpipe; Boots of stone

146.setup: Standard ranged setup carried for an extended stay: prayer potions/super restores and food (e.g. cooked moonlight antelope) for 15 consecutive kills.
146.strategy: Stamina/endurance task - do not leave the room between the 15 kills. Bring enough prayer restoration and food to outlast all 15 without banking; use correct vents to keep damage taken low.
146.items: Toxic blowpipe; Dragon hunter crossbow; Boots of stone

147.stats: 75+ Ranged, 43+ Prayer (recommend 70+ Defence, 61+ for Eagle Eye)
147.setup: Ranged: rune crossbow + diamond bolts (e), blowpipe, crystal bow, or twisted bow; full armour with high Magic defence is not needed since Jad has no def, prioritise prayer bonus and ranged accuracy/strength; Protect from Magic as default prayer
147.strategy: Stand at max range from Jad, watch his attack animation (feet slam = Ranged, rears up = Magic) and flick the correct overhead between attacks; kill the 4 Yt-HurKot healers when Jad drops below 50% (150 HP) before they restore him.

148.setup: Same ranged setup as a normal completion (rune crossbow/blowpipe/crystal bow/twisted bow, Protect from Magic default); consistency matters more than raw stats across 5 runs
148.strategy: This is a cumulative grind, not a single harder fight; complete the caves five times using consistent prayer-flicking and healer management on each run.

149.stats: 80+ Ranged, 43+ Prayer, 80+ Defence and high HP to survive an unprotected hit
149.setup: Ranged setup with as much HP/Defence as practical so an unprotected Jad hit (max ~97) is survivable; bring Saradomin brews
149.strategy: Tank a single Jad attack with the wrong overhead (do this at high HP, ideally his ranged attack), survive it, then re-enable correct prayers and finish the caves; do NOT die.

150.setup: High-DPS ranged (twisted bow/blowpipe ideal) to burst Yt-MejKot before they can heal; Protect from Melee when in their range
150.strategy: Yt-MejKot heal monsters below half HP including themselves; kill each Yt-MejKot quickly and keep them isolated so they never trigger a heal animation - high ranged DPS or finishing them before they drop low is the safest approach.

151.setup: High-DPS ranged with Rigour if available; bring extra ranging potions/brews to manage the healer phase
151.strategy: When Jad hits 150 HP, four Yt-HurKot spawn; lure/kill them away from Jad or kill them before any reaches melee range (they heal if within melee of Jad or within 4 tiles while fighting you). Attack the healers immediately so none channel a heal into Jad.

152.setup: Ranged setup; kill Tz-Kih at distance and use safespots/run tactics to avoid their melee
152.strategy: Tz-Kih (lvl 22 bats) drain prayer on a successful hit; kill them first from range each wave and hit-and-run so none ever land an attack on you for the whole run.

153.setup: Melee: best available weapon (e.g. Osmumten's fang/Ghrazi rapier/Abyssal whip + defender or Saradomin godsword), Bandos/Justiciar style tank armour, Piety; Protect from Magic against Jad
153.strategy: Melee-only forces you next to Jad so his instant melee attack also threatens you; flick Protect from Melee/Missiles/Magic correctly while standing adjacent. Saradomin godsword specs help sustain HP and prayer. Much harder than a ranged run.

154.setup: Ranged setup relying on perfect prayer flicking (1-tick flicking overheads) instead of leaving prayers on; no offensive prayers that drain
154.strategy: You must flick every protection prayer (never leaving it on) and never get hit by a Tz-Kih, so prayer never drops below your starting level. Requires precise tick-perfect flicking; many use a metronome/timing. Extremely demanding.

155.setup: High-DPS ranged (twisted bow or blowpipe + Rigour) to clear waves faster
155.strategy: Maximise DPS each wave and avoid time-wasting safespotting; aggressively clear spawns and burst Jad. A clean ranged run with good gear comfortably beats 30 minutes.

156.setup: Top-tier ranged DPS (twisted bow + Rigour, or blowpipe) and optimised wave clearing
156.strategy: Tighter speed target than the 30-minute task; needs efficient movement, minimal supply downtime, and high sustained DPS to shave the extra ~3.5 minutes. Best gear and a practised route required.

157.setup: Phase 1 (crawling): melee, crush weapon, protect from Magic/Missiles isn't needed but pray Melee off if hit; Phase 2 (airborne): ranged (blowpipe/bowfa) or fire spells (fire weakness) with Protect from Melee.
157.strategy: Plain kill count - just farm 25 kills. KQ switches forms: melee her crawling form, then swap to ranged/magic for the airborne form. Bring Saradomin brews/restores; her magic bounces and ranged drains prayer, so keep prayer topped up.

158.setup: Same hybrid setup as Adept: crush melee for phase 1, ranged or fire magic for phase 2. Strong gear (Voidwaker/fang for melee, blowpipe/bowfa or tome of fire + harmonised for magic) speeds the 50-kill grind.
158.strategy: Plain kill count to 50 - efficiency matters. Use a banshee-style trip: brews + restores, prayer flick form switches, and consider an alt to share KQ in the multi area for faster kills.

159.stats: 80+ Strength/Attack, 80+ Magic (if using Accursed sceptre), 70+ Prayer, 85+ Hitpoints
159.setup: Bring a Defence-draining special weapon: Bandos godsword (most reliable), Dragon warhammer, Arclight/Emberlight, or Accursed sceptre. Defence drains from form 1 carry over to form 2.
159.strategy: Land a Defence-lowering spec (e.g. BGS or DWH) as one of your last actions, then finish the kill yourself without anyone else lowering her Defence afterwards. Soloing makes this trivial; in a team be the one who specs last.
159.items: Bandos godsword; Dragon warhammer; Arclight; Emberlight; Accursed sceptre; Dragon mace; Bone dagger

160.stats: 94 Magic (Vengeance), 40+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 85+ Hitpoints; Lunar Diplomacy completed
160.setup: Lunar spellbook with Vengeance active; runes for Vengeance (10 Astral, 2 Death, 10 Earth). Bring Protect from Melee for the airborne form and food to survive a hit.
160.strategy: Cast Vengeance on yourself, then deliberately take a KQ hit when she is at very low HP so the reflected damage (75% of the hit) lands the finishing blow. Tank KQ down to under ~23 HP first (max hit 31 x 0.75 ~ 23), then let her hit you to trigger the kill via Vengeance.
160.items: Cape of Accomplishment options for Lunar spellbook access; Lunar staff (any magic weapon); Astral rune; Death rune; Earth rune

161.setup: Verac's flail (crush) equipped throughout; ideally full Verac's set for the set effect (chance to ignore Defence/prayer). Protect from Melee for the airborne form, brews and restores.
161.strategy: Verac's flail is crush and its set effect can bypass Defence, helping against KQ's high Defence on both forms. Since you cannot switch weapons, you must melee the airborne (magic) form too - keep Protect from Melee up and tank the slow kill on phase 2.

162.setup: Anti-dragon/dragonfire shield + antifire potion, melee with crush/slash weapon (e.g. abyssal whip or DDS), Protect from Melee. Ranged with crossbow + diamond bolts also works.
162.strategy: Plain kill count: stand under the dragon, pray Protect from Melee, keep an antifire potion active and shield equipped so dragonfire does no damage. 10 kills total.
162.items: Anti-dragon shield (or Dragonfire shield); Antifire potion; Antidote/antipoison (for poison breath)

163.setup: Anti-dragon/dragonfire shield + antifire potion, melee crush/slash weapon + Protect from Melee, or ranged crossbow setup. Bring extra food/prayer potions for the longer grind.
163.strategy: Same method as Novice but 25 kills total; consider a private instance to avoid PKers in the Wilderness lair while accumulating kills.
163.items: Anti-dragon shield (or Dragonfire shield); Antifire potion; Antidote/antipoison

164.stats: 43+ Prayer (required for Protect from Melee), 70+ melee or Ranged, 70+ Hitpoints
164.setup: Anti-dragon/dragonfire shield + antifire potion, prayer potions to sustain Protect from Melee, melee crush/slash weapon.
164.strategy: Activate Protect from Melee before landing the kill (KBD's melee max is 25, so this prayer is the natural way to fight it anyway). Keep prayer points topped up so it stays active when the dragon dies.
164.items: Anti-dragon shield (or Dragonfire shield); Antifire potion; Antidote/antipoison

165.setup: Stab weapon set to a stab attack option (e.g. Ghrazi rapier on Lunge, Zamorakian hasta on Lunge, or Dragon dagger), anti-dragon shield + antifire potion, Protect from Melee.
165.strategy: KBD has higher stab defence (70) than slash/crush (90 each is its strongest) so stab is actually its lowest defence value here — still, ensure your weapon's attack style is set to stab; a whip cannot stab, so bring a proper stab weapon.
165.items: A stab weapon (e.g. Dragon dagger / Dragon dagger(p++), Abyssal dagger, Ghrazi rapier, Zamorakian hasta, Dragon hasta, Leaf-bladed sword, or whip set to Lunge/Stab); Anti-dragon shield (or Dragonfire shield); Antifire potion

166.setup: Anti-dragon shield specifically (NOT dragonfire shield), antifire potion sipped to keep the timer running, melee crush/slash + Protect from Melee.
166.strategy: Use an anti-dragon shield (a dragonfire shield does not count for this task's wording) and keep the antifire potion timer up - re-sip before it runs out so both conditions hold when the dragon dies.
166.items: Antifire potion (regular or extended/super antifire); Anti-dragon shield

167.setup: Anti-dragon/dragonfire shield + antifire potion, strong melee crush/slash weapon or ranged crossbow, Protect from Melee, plenty of food and prayer potions since you cannot restock without leaving.
167.strategy: Pay 50k via the lever's right-click Private option, then grind all 10 kills in one trip - bring enough supplies (sharks/brews + prayer pots) because leaving the instance to restock resets the task. A bank/ironman scroll cannot be used inside, so over-stock.
167.items: 50,000 coins (private instance fee); Anti-dragon shield (or Dragonfire shield); Antifire potion; Antidote/antipoison; Food and prayer potions to last 10 kills

168.stats: 87 Slayer (hard req), 50+ Magic (req), 75+ Magic recommended, 43+ Prayer
168.setup: Magic only. Trident of the seas/swamp (or Sang/Shadow), occult necklace, tome of water/ancestral, imbued heart. Eat through hits (no useful protection prayer).
168.strategy: Disturb all 4 whirlpools or use a fishing explosive on the main whirlpool to wake the Kraken, then attack it with Magic; repeat for 20 kills. Use an instance to reset quickly between kills.
168.items: Magic weapon (Trident of the seas, Trident of the swamp, Sanguinesti staff, or Tumeken's shadow)

169.setup: Magic only. Trident/Sang/Shadow, occult necklace, magic damage gear, imbued heart. Spec/burst weapons optional to speed up tentacle clears.
169.strategy: After waking the Kraken, do NOT focus the boss; first kill each of the four level-112 tentacles, then finish the Kraken. Tentacles are accurate but die fast to Magic; clear them while tanking the boss's typeless hits.
169.items: Magic weapon (Trident of the seas, Trident of the swamp, Sanguinesti staff, or Tumeken's shadow)

170.setup: Magic only. Trident of the seas/swamp (Blowpipe-style efficiency not possible), occult necklace, magic gear; bring plenty of food/prayer potions since you cannot restock.
170.strategy: Pay 25k for a private instance and stay inside for all 25 kills - leaving the room resets progress. Bring enough supplies (food, runes/charges, restore pots) to last; the boss respawns slower in instances so expect a longer grind.
170.items: Magic weapon (Trident of the seas, Trident of the swamp, Sanguinesti staff, or Tumeken's shadow); Kraken instance fee (25,000 coins); Food

171.setup: Magic only. Trident of the swamp/Sang/Shadow, occult necklace, ancestral or magic damage gear, imbued heart; large food + prayer potion supply.
171.strategy: Stay in one instance for all 50 kills without leaving. Bring a deep supply of food and charges/runes; consider high magic damage gear to shorten each kill since the instanced respawn is already slow.
171.items: Magic weapon (Trident of the seas, Trident of the swamp, Sanguinesti staff, or Tumeken's shadow); Kraken instance fee (25,000 coins); Food

172.setup: Magic only. Best available magic weapon (Tumeken's shadow / Sanguinesti / Trident of the swamp), occult necklace, ancestral robes, imbued heart; maximum food/prayer/charge supply to last 100 kills.
172.strategy: Commit to one instance for all 100 kills - do not leave the room or progress resets. Take the largest possible supply of food, restore potions and weapon charges/runes; the slow instanced respawn makes this a long uninterrupted grind, so high DPS magic gear helps a lot.
172.items: Magic weapon (Trident of the seas, Trident of the swamp, Sanguinesti staff, or Tumeken's shadow); Kraken instance fee (25,000 coins); Large food and supply stock

173.setup: Leaf-bladed weapon (sword/battleaxe/spear) for melee, or broad bolts on a crossbow for ranged; standard melee/ranged armour. Magic Dart via Slayer's staff also works.
173.strategy: Kurask is immune to all attacks except leaf-bladed weapons, broad ammunition, and the Magic Dart spell. Bring one of those or you will deal 0 damage. It is inaccurate so prayer is optional; protect from melee if the superior (Kurask superior) spawns. Found in the Fremennik Slayer Dungeon at 70 Slayer.
173.items: Leaf-bladed sword; Leaf-bladed battleaxe; Leaf-bladed spear; Broad bolts; Broad arrows; Amethyst broad bolts; Slayer's staff (Magic Dart)

174.setup: Ranged (crossbow/bow) with Protect from Missiles; Shayzien armour (tier 5 ideal) to negate acid; stand at distance
174.strategy: Stand a few tiles away to avoid the diagonal melee, pray Protect from Missiles for the ranged attack, and move off the highlighted tile to dodge the jump/spawn AoE while DPSing it down.

175.setup: High-DPS Ranged (e.g. Toxic blowpipe or Bow of Faerdhinen/rune+ crossbow) with Protect from Missiles; full Shayzien tier 5 to negate acid; brews/Ranging pots
175.strategy: Pray Protect from Missiles to block its ranged hits and keep moving off the highlighted tiles to dodge the jump attack; full Shayzien tier 5 negates the acid attack. Burst it down fast so none of its attacks land - only the shaman's own damage breaks the task, spawn damage is allowed. Easiest solo in the Molch temple where you can isolate one shaman.

176.stats: 70+ Magic, 70+ Ranged, 43+ Prayer, 70+ Defence (higher is much easier)
176.setup: Magic is the Mimic's weakness: use ice spells (Ice Barrage/Entangle) to freeze it, or a Toxic blowpipe; bring Protect from Melee and stat-boost potions
176.strategy: Just need one kill. Use Protect from Melee throughout, freeze the Mimic with ice spells to avoid its melee, step out of the way of the six thrown candies, never stand under the Mimic (stomp damage scales up to 6/tick), and ignore the Third Age minions.

177.setup: Melee: Slash/crush weapon (e.g. abyssal whip or Saradomin sword), high Strength bonus gear, Protect from Melee
177.strategy: Just kill the mole 10 times. Use a spade to enter via mole hills beneath Falador Park and bring a bullseye lantern (closed light source the mole cannot extinguish); Protect from Melee negates all of her damage.

178.setup: Melee: high Strength gear with abyssal whip/Saradomin sword, or crush weapon vs her higher slash/crush defence; Protect from Melee
178.strategy: Kill the mole 25 times; a Falador Shield 3+ in inventory shows a flashing arrow to track her after burrowing, greatly speeding up grinding the kill count.

179.stats: 75+ Attack, 80+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer (or 85+ Ranged with Twisted bow)
179.setup: Highest DPS available: Twisted bow / max melee with Protect from Melee to burn her through the 50%-5% HP window fast
179.strategy: Burrows only roll between 50% and 5% HP (25% chance per hit), and she cannot burrow below 10 HP. Bring the highest DPS gear to blitz through that window so fewer than 3 burrow rolls land; cannon/thralls help. A Falador shield 3 helps relocate her if she does burrow.
179.items: Spade; Light source (e.g. bullseye lantern); Falador shield 3 (recommended)

180.stats: 75+ Attack, 80+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer (or 85+ Ranged, 77+ Prayer)
180.setup: Protect from Melee active the entire fight; high DPS melee or Twisted bow
180.strategy: Keep Protect from Melee on at all times - it negates all of her melee damage, so the only hits you take are when the prayer lapses (e.g. while relocating after a burrow). React quickly after each burrow and re-flick the prayer; staying close avoids extra hits.

181.stats: 75+ Attack, 80+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer (or 85+ Ranged with Twisted bow)
181.setup: High burst DPS: Twisted bow or max melee spec weapons; Protect from Melee
181.strategy: Bring her low (below ~50% so a burrow is likely), bank a special-attack/DPS burst, then chase her down after she resurfaces and kill her within 10 seconds. A Falador shield 3 flashing arrow finds her instantly so you do not waste the window searching.
181.items: Spade; Light source (e.g. bullseye lantern); Falador shield 3 (recommended)

182.setup: Protect from Melee throughout; standard melee DPS gear
182.strategy: Keep Protect from Melee active for the whole fight - it negates all of her melee damage, so she deals zero. Alternatively use a safespot against a rock/cavern wall (her 3x3 size lets you avoid her attacks). Do this solo so no other player takes a hit.

183.setup: Crush melee (Inquisitor's mace / Zamorakian hasta / Osmumten's fang / Abyssal bludgeon), Bandos/Inquisitor's armour, Protect from Magic + Piety; can be done in a team to lower the bar
183.strategy: Plain kill count for 1 kill. Pray against the boss's current attack style, break the shield then charge each of the four totems; group scaling makes a first kill far easier than solo.

184.setup: Crush melee (Scythe of Vitur > Inquisitor's mace / Soulreaper axe; budget Zamorakian hasta / Osmumten's fang), Bandos/Inquisitor's, Protect from Magic + Piety, Saradomin brews + restores
184.strategy: 25 kills - pure attrition. Learn shield-break timing and totem rotation for consistent kills; teams clear faster and let you bank the KC quickly.

185.setup: Crush melee (Scythe of Vitur / Inquisitor's mace / Soulreaper axe), Bandos/Inquisitor's, Protect from Magic + Piety, brews/restores; team kills to grind volume
185.strategy: 50 kills - long grind. Efficient gear and prayer flicking against ranged/magic phases reduces supply use so you sustain repeated trips.

186.setup: High-DPS crush melee (Scythe of Vitur / Soulreaper axe / Inquisitor's mace), full Inquisitor's, Protect from Magic + Piety, brews/restores
186.strategy: Solo. When a parasite is about to burst, walk underneath the Nightmare to immobilise it, then kill it before it reaches and heals the boss - keep total healing under 100 across the whole fight.

187.setup: Crush melee with high burst, or a spec weapon to even out Husk HP; Protect from Magic + Piety
187.strategy: Husks (sleepwalker remnants) must die on the same tick. Damage both down to roughly equal low HP, then land near-simultaneous hits (AoE/burst or coordinated team damage) so they explode together.

188.setup: Crush melee, full defensive set-up, Protect from Magic/Missiles/Melee swapped to match the boss, Piety; solo or a disciplined small team
188.strategy: Perfection run: keep the correct overhead prayer up at all times (off-prayer hits are +20% and disqualify), step out of rift zones and the surge lane, cure/kill parasites before they explode, avoid corpse flowers, and stand clear of spores so no attack gets slowed.

189.setup: Crush melee (Scythe of Vitur / Inquisitor's mace / Osmumten's fang), full Inquisitor's/Bandos, Protect from Magic + Piety, Saradomin brews + restores
189.strategy: Solo kill - all 2,400 HP plus shield is on you. Bring plenty of brews/restores, pray correctly each attack-style swap, and manage parasites yourself.

190.stats: 85+ Attack/Strength/Defence, 85+ Hitpoints, Prayer level high enough but kept full
190.setup: Crush melee, no overhead prayers used; rely on positioning and food only (NO offensive prayers either)
190.strategy: Complete the fight without activating any prayer at all - eat through hits and dodge mechanics manually. Easiest in a team so the boss dies fast before damage becomes unsustainable; avoid all prayer (including Piety) so prayer points never drop.

191.setup: High-DPS crush melee (Scythe of Vitur ideal), Inquisitor's, Protect from Magic + Piety, brews/restores
191.strategy: Solo speed (lenient 23-min cap). Maintain near-constant DPS, break shields efficiently and charge totems without downtime; a Scythe makes the timer comfortable.

192.setup: BIS crush melee (Scythe of Vitur / Soulreaper axe), full Inquisitor's, Protect from Magic + Piety, brews/restores
192.strategy: Solo speed (19 min). Needs strong sustained DPS - Scythe, perfect prayer swaps and tight totem routing to avoid wasted ticks.

193.setup: Top-tier crush melee (Scythe of Vitur, full Inquisitor's, max strength bonuses), Protect from Magic + Piety, optimal supplies
193.strategy: Solo speed (tight 16 min). Requires near-max DPS gear and flawless mechanics - constant attacking, instant shield breaks and totem charges with zero idle ticks.

194.setup: Crush melee per player (Scythe of Vitur / Inquisitor's mace), coordinated DPS, Protect from Magic + Piety
194.strategy: 5-man team speed (lenient 5 min). Stack DPS, all players break shields together and split totem charging; with five competent meleers the timer is easy.

195.setup: BIS crush melee on all five (Scythe of Vitur preferred), coordinated shield breaks and totem rotation, Protect from Magic + Piety
195.strategy: 5-man team speed (4 min). Needs a competent, geared team hitting hard simultaneously and charging all four totems quickly with minimal mistakes.

196.setup: Five maxed crush meleers (Scythe of Vitur, full Inquisitor's), tight coordination, Protect from Magic + Piety
196.strategy: 5-man team speed (very tight 3:30). Requires a fully geared, well-practised team with optimal DPS, instant shield breaks and flawless simultaneous totem charges.

197.setup: Ranged - rune/dragon crossbow or toxic blowpipe with Protect from Magic; isolate Prime in the NE corner
197.strategy: Plain kill count: keep Protect from Magic on at all times (Prime hits hard with magic) and stand in the north-eastern corner to avoid Rex and Supreme aggro while ranging Prime down 10 times.

198.setup: Ranged - dragon/rune crossbow, blowpipe or higher (Bow of Faerdhinen/Twisted bow) with Protect from Magic
198.strategy: Same as the Medium version but 25 kills - bring extra prayer potions/food and Protect from Magic for sustained trips; isolate Prime in the NE corner to extend the trip.

199.setup: High defence bulk-up gear (e.g. Spectral spirit shield, high Defence) so Rex/Supreme have low accuracy; bring ranged for Prime plus melee or stay tanky vs Rex/Supreme
199.strategy: Deliberately stand where all three Kings can hit you (do not isolate Prime). Keep Protect from Magic up for Prime; with very high Defence, Rex and Supreme only land 15-30% of hits. Kill Prime last so its final hit lands while Supreme and Rex are still aggroed onto you.

200.setup: Ranged setup to chip Prime low, plus a Rune thrownaxe in inventory/weapon slot for the special attack; Protect from Magic for Prime
200.strategy: Bring Prime to 1-2 HP first, then equip the Rune thrownaxe and use its special attack (Sever, 25% energy) targeting Dagannoth Rex while Prime is adjacent - the spec bounces off Rex onto Prime to land the killing blow. Position Prime and Rex next to each other so the bounce can reach Prime.

201.setup: Magic: Iban Blast / earth spells / powered staff (Trident), Barrows or other defensive body/legs, Protect from Melee on.
201.strategy: Rex is melee-only and weak to magic (35% extra from earth spells); cast earth-based magic while praying Protect from Melee. Lure to the south-east corner of the lair so Prime and Supreme do not aggro, and just rack up 10 kills.

202.setup: Magic: Trident / earth surge / Iban Blast, Barrows or void/defensive armour, Protect from Melee. Bring food and an Ancients/standard switch for freezing.
202.strategy: Same as the 10-kill task but extended to 25 kills; safespot Rex away from the other kings in the SE corner and chip him down with earth magic under Protect from Melee. Consider banking trips via Waterbirth teleport or the dungeon for restocks.

203.setup: High-DPS magic on Rex (Trident/earth surge) with strong defensive armour (Barrows/Bandos hybrid). Cannot pray against all three styles, so emphasise Defence/HP and food; bring a Ring of Recoil and brews.
203.strategy: Aggro all three kings at once (stand centrally), then focus and finish Rex with magic while Supreme and Prime are still attacking you. Rex must die last (or while the other two live), so do not kill Prime/Supreme first; you cannot protect against melee, range and magic simultaneously, so tank with Defence/HP and food.

204.stats: 82+ Magic (Ice spells) or 79+ Magic (Entangle), 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer
204.setup: Magic with a freeze: Ancient spellbook (Ice Barrage/Blitz) or standard Entangle, plus earth-spell/Trident DPS. Defensive armour and Protect from Melee.
204.strategy: Freeze Rex with an ice spell (Ice Barrage/Blitz/Burst/Rush) or Entangle/Snare from a distance, then deal the final hit while the freeze timer is still active so he dies immobilized. Re-freeze as needed; line up the killing blow during a freeze window.
204.items: Ice spell (Ice Rush / Burst / Blitz / Barrage) or Entangle/Snare/Bind

205.setup: Two-style burst: magic (earth/Trident) for Rex plus a ranged weapon (e.g. blowpipe/bow) for Supreme, or a partner. Defensive armour and food; alternate attacks to even out their HP.
205.strategy: Bring both kings to very low HP, then alternate timed hits (or coordinate with a partner) so both die on the same tick. Easiest done duo: each player finishes a different king simultaneously. Rex is weak to magic (earth) and Supreme to ranged, so split styles accordingly to balance their final hits.

206.setup: Crush melee (Bandos godsword / Abyssal bludgeon) or Ranged with crossbow; Protect from Missiles/Melee prayer switching
206.strategy: Plain kill count of 10. Sarachnis is weak to crush and fire; melee her with a crush weapon adjacent and pray Protect from Melee, switching to Missiles when she webs.

207.setup: Crush melee (Abyssal bludgeon / Bandos godsword / Inquisitor's) with prayer switching, or chinning/crossbow Ranged
207.strategy: Plain kill count of 25; same fight as Novice. Bring Saradomin brews/restores for sustained trips and exploit her crush weakness for fast kills.

208.setup: Crush melee weapon (Abyssal bludgeon / Inquisitor's mace) to stay adjacent; high DPS to limit total attacks
208.strategy: She only uses ranged when you are out of melee range, so stay adjacent to force melee attacks; after she webs/relocates, immediately re-enter melee distance before her next attack so two ranged hits never occur back-to-back.

209.setup: Crush melee with overhead prayers (Protect from Melee + Missiles switching), high DPS gear (Inquisitor's set / bludgeon)
209.strategy: Pray Protect from Melee while adjacent; when she shoots the web flick to Protect from Missiles to block the queued ranged hit, then close back to melee. Kill the spawns at 66%/33% (or pray correctly) so they deal no damage either.

210.setup: Any crush weapon set to crush attack style; Sarachnis cudgel, Abyssal bludgeon, Inquisitor's mace, BGS or granite maul all work
210.strategy: Sarachnis is weak to crush (defence-crush only 10), so this is the optimal style anyway. Equip a crush weapon on crush attack mode and kill her normally with prayer switching.
210.items: Abyssal bludgeon; Inquisitor's mace; Bandos godsword; Sarachnis cudgel; Granite maul; Zamorakian hasta (crush mode)

211.setup: Ranged: blowpipe/tbow with Protect from Melee and Eagle Eye; or melee with whip/scythe under Protect from Melee. Bring food and prayer/restore potions.
211.strategy: Plain 50-kill grind. Take the central tile, run her into melee range so she stops casting accurate magic, and pray Protect from Melee while dealing with the three bodyguards (kill Bree first to cut ranged damage).
211.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Ranged weapon (Toxic blowpipe / Twisted bow / Bow of faerdhinen); Saradomin brews; Super restores; Prayer potions

212.setup: Ranged: blowpipe/tbow with Protect from Melee + Eagle Eye; or melee whip/scythe. Bank gear for efficient trips between kills.
212.strategy: Same fight as the 50 KC task, just doubled to 100 kills. Use the centre tile and Protect from Melee; clear bodyguards each kill (priority Bree, then Growler) and chug a brew/restore when low.
212.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Ranged weapon (Toxic blowpipe / Twisted bow / Bow of faerdhinen); Saradomin brews; Super restores; Prayer potions

213.setup: Ranged/melee setup with high single-target DPS; an instance helps so you control aggro and timing.
213.strategy: Kill all three bodyguards first while keeping Zilyana low but not dead, then finish her once the room is clear. Easiest in a rented instance: nuke the bodyguards, then dump DPS into Zilyana for the final blow.
213.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Ranged weapon (Toxic blowpipe / Twisted bow); Saradomin brews; Super restores

214.setup: Max ranged (tbow/blowpipe), full prayer-bonus armour, and freezes (ancient magicks/ice spells or a bind) to keep mobs immobile while you stay out of range.
214.strategy: Pray Protect from Magic and stay at distance so Zilyana cannot cast (she only uses magic at melee range). Lure and freeze the bodyguards: keep Bree (ranged) and Growler (magic) frozen or LoS-blocked, and never let Starlight or Zilyana reach melee distance. Use pillars to break line of sight and reset positions between attacks.
214.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Coins (instance fee); Toxic blowpipe / Twisted bow / Bow of faerdhinen; Saradomin brews; Super restores; Prayer potions

215.setup: Damage-over-time / indirect sources: recoil ring, summoned thralls, poison/venom, and AoE that hits her while you target a bodyguard.
215.strategy: Never click Zilyana. Whittle her down with indirect damage: recoil ring (she hits you, takes recoil), conjured thralls auto-attacking her, venom/poison, and AoE weapons (chinchompas or scythe) aimed at adjacent bodyguards so the splash hits her. Do this in an instance so you can stack thralls and reposition safely.
215.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Coins (instance fee); Ring of recoil / Ring of suffering (recoil); Chinchompas or scythe of vitur (AoE off bodyguards); Ghostly/superior thralls (runes + Arceuus spellbook); Saradomin brews; Super restores

216.stats: 80+ Attack, 80+ Strength, 80+ Defence, 75+ HP, 70 Agility, 44+ Prayer
216.setup: Best melee weapon (scythe/rapier/whip), high-strength gear, Protect from Melee, and brews for sustain at melee distance.
216.strategy: Stay in melee range and pray Protect from Melee. Note Zilyana switches to magic (min hit 10) at melee distance, so expect chip damage; scythe is strong here since it also cleaves the bodyguards. Kill the bodyguards (Bree first) then finish Zilyana, all with melee only.
216.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Coins (instance fee); Scythe of vitur / Abyssal whip / Ghrazi rapier; Bandos/Torva melee armour; Saradomin brews; Super restores

217.setup: High-DPS ranged setup with maximum-efficiency supplies: stack Saradomin brews, super restores and prayer potions to last all 50 kills without restocking.
217.strategy: Pay for a private instance and grind 50 kills without exiting. Bring a full inventory of brews/restores (and a looting bag isn't needed since you can drop loot); manage HP with brews and restore prayer/stats so you never need to bank. Efficient prayer flicking extends supplies.
217.items: Ecumenical key (or 40 Saradomin killcount); Rope; Coins (instance fee); Toxic blowpipe / Twisted bow / Bow of faerdhinen; Saradomin brews; Super restores; Prayer potions; Stamina potions

218.setup: Magic with Ancient Magicks freezes; Accursed/Thammaron's sceptre or trident, Bloodbark/mystic armour, Occult necklace, imbued god cape, Protect from Missiles vs offspring
218.strategy: Deep-Wilderness multi-combat boss, so bring no untradeables you cannot lose and watch for PKers; freeze Scorpia with Ice spells, attack from range with magic, and bank 10 kills.

219.setup: Magic with Ancient Magicks freezes; Accursed/Thammaron's sceptre or trident, Bloodbark armour, Occult necklace, imbued god cape, Protect from Missiles vs offspring
219.strategy: Same fight as Adept but 25 kills means longer Wilderness exposure to PKers; keep wealth minimal, carry a stamina/teleport escape, and freeze her to whittle kills out safely.

220.setup: Magic with Ice Barrage/Blitz freezes (Ancient Magicks), Occult necklace, imbued god cape, Protect from Missiles; stay outside her melee range
220.strategy: Stay out of melee range and keep Scorpia frozen with Ice spells while DPSing her with magic; pray Protect from Missiles so the offspring's ranged hits don't break the no-damage run.

221.setup: Magic with Ancient Magicks freezes (Ice Barrage/Blitz) for crowd control; Occult necklace, imbued god cape, Protect from Missiles
221.strategy: When the two guardians spawn at <50% HP, freeze both and lure Scorpia more than 3 tiles away; the guardians despawn after ~15s out of healing range, letting you finish her off without killing them.

222.setup: Magic main-damage (trident of swamp/seas, occult necklace, ancestral/Ahrim's, seers ring, tome of water) plus a fast off-weapon (toxic blowpipe/chinchompas/MSB) to instantly pop the healing beams. Augury + Protect from Missiles.
222.strategy: Damage to her is capped ~22-24 per hit, so the 4-min limit is tight: maintain constant magic DPS, kill the true clone fast, and snipe every healing beam the instant it spawns (blood spells/blowpipe) so none of her HP regenerates. Pre-pot brews/super restores and bring overloads/divine potions to never miss a tick to manual eating.
222.items: Trident of the swamp; Trident of the seas; Occult necklace; Ancestral robes; Saradomin brew; Super restore; Toxic blowpipe

223.setup: Anti-wyvern shield (elemental/mind/dragonfire/ancient wyvern) plus either a Dragon hunter crossbow with enchanted dragonstone/diamond bolts, or an Abyssal whip; Protect from Missiles where not safespotting.
223.strategy: Just a single kill. You MUST wield one of the wyvern shields (elemental/mind/dragonfire/dragonfire ward/ancient wyvern) to reduce the icy breath to 10 damage and to be assigned them; ranged with a DHCB is easiest, or safespot to avoid melee/ranged. Located in the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon (72 Slayer needed).
223.items: Elemental shield (or Mind shield / Dragonfire shield / Dragonfire ward / Ancient wyvern shield)

224.setup: Magic-and-Ranged switch: Toxic blowpipe + crystal/Armadyl for ranged phases; Trident of the swamp + ancestral/Ahrim's for magic phases. Serpentine helm for venom immunity.
224.strategy: Learn the four set rotations (and the random recurring rotation) so you swap to Magic on green phase and Ranged on blue/red; flick Protect from Magic/Ranged accordingly to reach 25 kills.
224.items: Anti-venom+; Toxic blowpipe; Trident of the swamp

225.setup: Magic-and-Ranged switch: Toxic blowpipe + crystal/Armadyl, Trident of the swamp + ancestral. Serpentine helm or anti-venom for venom.
225.strategy: Sustained 75 kills rewards memorising rotations to minimise misclicks; bank trips reduced by good food (sharks/manta) and prayer pots.
225.items: Anti-venom+; Toxic blowpipe; Trident of the swamp

226.setup: Magic-and-Ranged switch with high-end gear: Toxic blowpipe + crystal/masori, Trident of the swamp + ancestral. Serpentine helm for venom.
226.strategy: 150 kills is endurance; full rotation mastery and efficient prayer flicking keep supplies low enough to chain kills.
226.items: Anti-venom+; Toxic blowpipe; Trident of the swamp

227.stats: 94 Magic (for Vengeance), Lunar Diplomacy completed, 75+ Ranged
227.setup: Lunar spellbook for Vengeance; standard ranged/magic Zulrah gear otherwise. Cast Vengeance, then deliberately tank a Zulrah hit when it is low HP.
227.strategy: Bring Zulrah to very low HP, cast Vengeance, then let Zulrah hit you so the reflected damage deals the finishing blow rather than your own attack.
227.items: Vengeance spell (Lunar spellbook); Lunar staff or equivalent; Astral rune; Death rune; Earth rune

228.setup: Standard Zulrah ranged gear; optionally chinchompas for splash damage, but single-target stacking works since snakelings share low HP.
228.strategy: Wait until three snakelings have spawned and lined up, then hit so all three die on the same tick; ignoring them earlier so multiple are alive at once makes this easier.
228.items: Toxic blowpipe; Magic shortbow (i) or chinchompas (optional AoE)

229.stats: 85+ Ranged, 85+ Magic, 45+ Prayer, perfect rotation knowledge
229.setup: Full switch gear with Serpentine helm; correct Protect prayers up at all times and pre-emptive movement to dodge venom clouds and the crimson tail.
229.strategy: Flick Protect from Ranged on green and step two tiles from the crimson melee tail; kill snakelings before they hit and never stand in a venom cloud. Damage from the blue phase does not fail the task.
229.items: Anti-venom+; Serpentine helm; Toxic blowpipe; Trident of the swamp

230.stats: 85+ Ranged, 85+ Magic, max melee for red phase, 44+ Prayer
230.setup: Best-in-slot DPS switches (Tumeken's shadow/Trident + ancestral for magic, masori + blowpipe/bowfa for ranged). Maximise per-phase damage with correct prayers and gear swaps.
230.strategy: Hit max DPS each phase with full prayer and gear switches; do not take a Zulrah Slayer task, as task-boosted kills do not count. Sub-1:20 is achievable with solid switches.
230.items: Toxic blowpipe; Trident of the swamp; Anti-venom+

231.setup: Top-tier switches: Tumeken's shadow + ancestral for magic, masori (f) + bowfa/blowpipe for ranged, max melee for the crimson phase. Smite/Augury/Rigour for max output.
231.strategy: Requires near-perfect prayer flicks and gear switches every phase plus high gear (shadow strongly helps); avoid a Zulrah Slayer task so the kill counts.
231.items: Toxic blowpipe; Tumeken's shadow or Trident of the swamp; Anti-venom+

232.stats: 95+ Ranged, 99 Magic, max melee, 44+ Prayer, BiS gear
232.setup: Absolute best-in-slot: Tumeken's shadow + ancestral/torva-adjacent magic switch, masori (f) + bowfa/blowpipe for ranged, max melee for crimson. Perfect Rigour/Augury switching.
232.strategy: Sub-54s demands flawless execution: maximum DPS gear, instant prayer/gear switches, and ideally a favourable rotation; one of the hardest Speed CAs. No Zulrah Slayer task.
232.items: Toxic blowpipe; Tumeken's shadow; Masori armour (f); Crystal/Bow of faerdhelen; Anti-venom+

233.setup: Melee: abyssal whip or tentacle whip, Saradomin godsword or dragon dagger for spec; high-Defence armour, Protect from Magic active
233.strategy: Supreme uses ranged and is most weak to melee (range defence 550, melee defence 10). Whip him down on the west side; keep Protect from Magic up to soak Prime if it spawns. 10 kills is plain KC.

234.setup: Melee: abyssal/tentacle whip plus Saradomin godsword spec; high-Defence armour, food and Prayer potions, Protect from Magic
234.strategy: Same as the 10-kill task but 25 kills, so bring more supplies. Melee Supreme (weak to melee), Protect from Magic against Prime, safespot on the west wall to limit how many kings hit you at once.

235.stats: 85+ Defence, 80+ in attacking styles (Att/Str, Ranged, Magic), 70+ Prayer for switching
235.setup: Tribrid: melee weapon for Supreme (rapier/whip/scythe), plus ranged/mage gear; high-Defence armour (Torva/Masori mix), Protect from Magic vs Prime, brews/Prayer pots
235.strategy: Don't kill Prime or Rex first. Pull all three kings together, keep Prime and Rex alive and attacking you, then finish Supreme with melee last. Protect from Magic to survive Prime (hits up to 50). High Defence keeps Rex/Supreme accuracy low while you tank both.

236.setup: Best-in-slot tribrid: Scythe/rapier (Supreme), Twisted bow or Bow of Faerdhinen (Prime), Tumeken's shadow or Sanguinesti staff (Rex); spec weapons (DDS/SGS/Voidwaker); high-Defence armour, Spectral shield
236.strategy: Bring each king low on HP first (each weak to its style: melee Supreme, range Prime, mage Rex), then burst all three dead inside a 9-second window. Stagger your final hits and use special attacks to land near-simultaneous kills. Best done in a duo/group or with very high DPS to enable the rapid finish.

237.stats: 90+ Attack/Strength, 90+ Defence, 90+ Ranged, 80+ Magic, 90+ HP, 77 Prayer
237.setup: Scythe of Vitur or Ghrazi Rapier (melee), Twisted bow/Zaryte crossbow (ranged), Sanguinesti staff (magic); Justiciar/Bandos/crystal armour, Avernic defender, brews and Saradomin brews.
237.strategy: Pure kill-count grind: complete the full raid (Maiden, Bloat, Nylocas, Sotetseg, Xarpus, Verzik) 25 times. Learn each room's prayer switches and movement; bring scythe spec/blood fury for sustain. Trio/small-team learning is fine - no perfection required, just survive and finish.

238.stats: 95+ Attack/Strength, 90+ Defence, 95+ Ranged, 90+ Magic, 95+ HP, 77 Prayer
238.setup: Scythe of Vitur (melee), Twisted bow/Zaryte crossbow (ranged), Sanguinesti staff (magic); strong defensive armour (Justiciar/Torva), Avernic defender, full supplies.
238.strategy: 75 full completions - by this point you should have efficient prayer-switching and a consistent team. Same gear as Veteran; consistency and not dying matters more than speed since deaths cost time and supplies.

239.stats: 99 Strength, 95+ Attack/Defence, 99 Ranged, 94+ Magic, 95+ HP, 77 Prayer
239.setup: Best-in-slot: Scythe of Vitur, Twisted bow, Sanguinesti staff; Torva/Justiciar, Avernic defender, Lightbearer, full brew/restore inventory.
239.strategy: 150 completions - a long grind best done with a dedicated, experienced team. Optimise kill speed and minimise deaths to reduce supply cost over many runs.

240.setup: Scythe of Vitur strongly preferred (multi-hit DPS); melee with Avernic defender; bring a strong special weapon for burst.
240.strategy: Bloat alternates between a walking phase (vulnerable) and shutting down (invulnerable). You must deal enough DPS during the first two active phases to kill it before the third shutdown. Scythe is near-essential; pre-position for hits and avoid wasting active windows. Easier on smaller scales (lower HP).

241.setup: DPS to clear adds quickly (scythe/blowpipe/staff); avoid any freezing weapon/spell on the Nylos during Verzik P2.
241.strategy: During Verzik's second phase she spawns Nylocas adds; do not use Ice Barrage/freezes on them and ensure no teammate is hit by a Nylo. Kill or out-position the explosive Nylos and keep the team spread so the adds' attacks miss. All members must avoid Nylo damage for the whole Verzik kill.

242.setup: High HP and defensive gear to survive solo ball hits; brews/Saradomin brews to overheal; scythe/tbow for DPS.
242.strategy: Sotetseg's ball (the big white energy attack) is normally split by standing together to reduce damage. Here one person must solo-eat at least 3 balls (do not stack to share) and survive each - overheal with Saradomin brews beforehand since a solo ball hits hard. No deaths allowed all fight, so manage HP carefully and still avoid maze/tornado deaths.

243.stats: 99 Strength, 95+ Attack/Defence, 99 Ranged, 94+ Magic, 95+ HP, 77 Prayer
243.setup: Best-in-slot per style: Scythe of Vitur, Twisted bow, Sanguinesti staff; Torva/Justiciar, Avernic defender, Lightbearer; Dawnbringer for Verzik P1; full supplies.
243.strategy: The capstone GM task: all six Perfect tasks in a single deathless run. Every mechanic must be executed flawlessly by the whole team - perfect prayer, no avoidable damage, correct Nylo styles, perfect maze/ball, exhumed control, and the Dawnbringer-only Verzik P1. Practise each Perfect individually first; run a coordinated experienced team. Bring Dawnbringer for Verzik phase 1.

244.setup: Scythe (melee) or ranged for the Maiden plus DPS to delete the red Nylos (blowpipe/crossbow); keep correct overhead prayers up.
244.strategy: Pray correctly against the Maiden's attacks (off-prayer hits fail the task), step off blood tiles/trails and dodge the blood spawn projectiles, and burst down every Nylocas Matomenos before it reaches and heals the Maiden. Spread the team to handle reds at 70/50/30% spawns.

245.setup: Scythe of Vitur for fast damage in the active windows; melee gear, Avernic defender.
245.strategy: Move continuously to avoid the falling body-part splats, stay out of the pestilent fly clouds, and back off when Bloat begins its stomp/down animation. Only attack during the walking phase and reposition before he shuts down. No DPS check here - pure avoidance for the whole team.

246.setup: All three styles ready for instant swaps: Scythe/rapier (melee), blowpipe/tbow (ranged), Sanguinesti staff/trident (magic); fast weapon switches to match Nylo colours.
246.strategy: Attack each Nylo only with its correct style (green=ranged-coloured etc. - hitting wrong style heals it 100%), defend all four pillars so none collapse, and keep the correct overhead prayer up against the boss Vasilias to avoid any off-prayer hit. Strong style-switching and add control is essential; smaller scales reduce add spawn pressure.

247.setup: Scythe/tbow for DPS; correct overhead prayers vs magic/ranged attacks; no special items.
247.strategy: The maze: the chosen player relays the red-tile path and everyone follows it exactly without stepping on a wrong tile. Dodge the tornado that chases a player, and keep the correct overhead prayer up at all times so no attack lands off-prayer. Communicate the maze path clearly for the whole team.

248.setup: Scythe/tbow for fast clears; melee/ranged gear. No special items.
248.strategy: During the exhumed (P1) phase, soak/destroy the exhumed pools so they heal Xarpus at most twice, then in the staring phase avoid all of his poison/projectile attacks (watch his gaze and step out of attacks). Take zero damage from Xarpus while keeping exhumed heals <=2.

249.setup: Dawnbringer (for P1 nuke), full DPS for P2/P3 (scythe/tbow/sang), strong armour and prayers; precise movement.
249.strategy: Only damage allowed is from the spider (P3) form's normal magic/ranged attacks while praying the right overhead. Avoid everything else: P1 melee/range, P2 nylo/web mechanics, the green ball, lightning/electric attacks, and the tornadoes/yellow pools in P3. Use Dawnbringer to skip/burn P1 safely. The whole team must avoid all non-prayed damage.

250.setup: High-defence gear (Justiciar) so you can fight without protection prayers; scythe/ranged DPS; no prayers active.
250.strategy: Prayer points must never drop, so the simplest approach is to use NO prayers (no Protect-from / offensive prayers) for the whole Maiden fight and tank her hits with high defence/brews, while still killing the red Nylos and dodging blood. Alternatively keep prayer pinned at max - but using and instantly restoring is risky; running zero prayers is the standard interpretation.

251.setup: Scythe of Vitur or Ghrazi Rapier (melee only), Avernic defender; melee armour (Bandos/Torva).
251.strategy: Kill Xarpus using only melee weapons - no blowpipe/trident/staff. During the staring phase you must run in to melee between his attacks while still dodging them. Scythe makes the melee-only kill far faster; the 'dance' is timing your melee hits around his gaze/attacks.

252.setup: Barrows weapons only: Dharok's greataxe / Verac's flail / Torag's hammers / Guthan's spear (melee), Karil's crossbow (ranged), Ahrim's staff (magic), plus Dawnbringer for Verzik P1. Strong non-weapon gear allowed (armour, defenders).
252.strategy: Only Barrows weapons may be equipped (Dawnbringer is the only allowed exception, for Verzik P1). Bring the right Barrows weapon for each room - e.g. Karil's crossbow or Ahrim's staff for Nylocas style-matching, a melee Barrows weapon elsewhere. DPS is much lower than meta gear, so expect a long, careful run; smaller scale reduces add/HP pressure.
252.items: Dawnbringer; Dharok's greataxe; Verac's flail; Torag's hammers; Guthan's warspear; Ahrim's staff; Karil's crossbow

253.setup: Ghrazi Rapier or Bandos godsword (melee) instead of scythe, Twisted bow/Zaryte crossbow (ranged), Sanguinesti staff (magic); otherwise standard ToB gear.
253.strategy: Complete the whole raid without anyone equipping a Scythe of Vitur. Substitute Rapier/Blade of Saeldor for melee DPS and lean more on ranged (tbow) for Bloat/Maiden. Kill times are slower without scythe, so play tighter to avoid running low on supplies.

254.stats: 99 Strength, 95+ Attack/Defence, 99 Ranged, 94+ Magic, 95+ HP, 77 Prayer
254.setup: Best-in-slot: Scythe of Vitur, Twisted bow, Sanguinesti staff; Torva/Justiciar, Avernic defender, Lightbearer; brews/Saradomin brews/anglers.
254.strategy: Speedrun on Duo scale. Two players means high per-person HP on bosses, so maximise DPS uptime, perfect prayer/style switches, and minimise downtime between rooms. Bring strong special-attack weapons for burst (e.g. tbow + BGS/voidwaker) and overstock supplies to push without stopping.

261.setup: Magic: powered staff (Tumeken's shadow / Toxic trident / Sanguinesti) or air surge with full magic gear; Augury and high-healing food
261.strategy: Must be on a smoke devil Slayer task to deal damage. Fight with magic - it has 20% air weakness and far lower magic defence than ranged. Bring Stamina potions; just farm 20 kills, no special restriction. Cannon cannot be used in the boss room.

262.strategy: Its typeless attack cannot be blocked by protection prayers, so you must avoid attacks entirely. Burst it down fast (best done in a 2-player kill so combined DPS drops it before it gets attacks off), or keep stepping out of its attack range between its 1.2s attacks. Thralls and a powered staff maximise the chance it dies before hitting anyone.
262.items: Powered staff (Tumeken's shadow / Toxic trident / Sanguinesti) or strong air-spell setup; Thralls (greater/superior) for added burst damage

263.strategy: Only special attacks count - swap between several spec weapons (e.g. Dragon claws, Voidwaker, SGS, Dragon dagger) and wait/regen special energy rather than auto-attacking. Note its very high ranged and slash defence, so high-damage specs like claws or Voidwaker (which partly ignore defence) are best; bring Saradomin brews for sustain during downtime.
263.items: Special-attack weapon(s): e.g. Dragon claws, Voidwaker, Saradomin godsword, Burning claws, Dragon dagger, Magic shortbow (i); Saradomin brews / restore potions to refill special energy via no normal attacks

264.strategy: Just kill Venenatis 10 times; requires med Wildy Diary or a Venenatis Slayer task to enter the lair. She switches between ranged and magic every two moves and crushes (def crush only 10) for melee. Use a single-way spot or hop empty worlds to avoid PKers, and keep a looting bag.
264.items: Burning claw or Voidwaker (spec for finishing); Heavy ballista or Karil's crossbow (ranged setup); Anguish or Necklace of anguish; Ranger boots or Pegasian boots; Saradomin brew; Super restore; Looting bag

270.strategy: Do 'The Walk': step continuously along clear tiles in a snaking pattern to dodge acid pools and fireballs, only stopping briefly to fire. Blowpipe's fast speed makes hitting 12 times within one acid phase easiest.
