304.strategy: Cross the tightrope and complete the room while avoiding dealing the killing blow to any Deathly Mage or Deathly Ranger. Use single-target attacks on intended targets and route past the Deathly enemies; avoid AoE that could finish them.

305.stats: 88+ combat, 90+ Magic, high supplies, ability to clear extra content
305.setup: Strong DPS solo setup; bring enough supplies to clear point-rich rooms (combat rooms, full Olm) rather than rushing.
305.strategy: Points scale with HP killed and challenge; in a solo, take a layout with plenty of combat rooms, kill everything, do thieving/skilling for bonus points, and avoid deaths (deaths reduce points). Larger/harder solos yield more points.

306.stats: 90+ combat all styles, 80+ Prayer, strong DPS
306.setup: High-DPS solo Olm gear (tbow + Sang/shadow + scythe/fang) with overloads to burn the head fast.
306.strategy: Damage Olm's head enough during the penultimate phase to skip into the final phase before he can fire those specials, and prevent the left (mage) hand autoheal by keeping its HP suppressed. Essentially out-DPS the phase / rush the head.

307.setup: Ranged (blowpipe/tbow) or magic to control aggro; carry enough DPS to kill the baby quickly.
307.strategy: Kill or burn down the baby Muttadile fast before it swims to the meat tree to heal, and lure the adult away from / DPS through it so neither restores HP at the tree. Chopping the meat tree first removes the heal source.

308.setup: Ranged setup (blowpipe/tbow) to focus the Abyssal Portal; protection prayer vs Vespula's attacks.
308.strategy: Ignore Vespula and focus all damage on the Abyssal Portal; do not damage Vespula's flight enough to ground her. Killing the portal alone completes the task while she stays in the air. Watch for the Lux Grub/sting damage and keep prayer up.

309.setup: High-DPS ranged/magic (tbow, shadow/Sang); overloads/salts to maximise damage between teleports.
309.strategy: Burst Vasa down fast. He charges from the crystals then teleports; you must kill him before he completes his second teleport, so focus the crystal he absorbs, then DPS his main body hard. Scaling down (smaller team) and high DPS make this easier.

310.setup: Melee burst: scythe of vitur or fang, Avernic/Torva, with dragon warhammer specs to drop his Defence (first DWH spec is guaranteed to land on Tekton).
310.strategy: DWH-spec Tekton at the start to crush his Defence, then burst him before he resets to the anvil twice. He returns to the anvil to heal/buff; high melee DPS after the Defence drop kills him in time.

311.stats: 75+ Mining (to mine boulders) or just DPS, 70+ combat
311.setup: Any combat style works; bring a pickaxe (dragon pick ideal) to help clear/mine. Movement awareness matters more than gear.
311.strategy: Watch the shadow markers for falling rocks and step off them each tick while finishing the Stone Guardian. Get the killing blow during a moment when you are clear of any incoming rock tiles.

312.stats: 80+ combat all styles, 74+ Prayer, ample supplies
312.setup: Survivability-focused: defensive gear options, plenty of brews/restores, all 3 styles for rooms and Olm.
312.strategy: Play safe over fast; everyone uses correct prayers, keeps HP topped, and is careful at Olm. One death anywhere (any member) fails it, so coordinate and over-supply.

313.stats: 85+ combat all styles, 80+ Prayer, strong supplies
313.setup: Reliable solo gear in all 3 styles with brews/restores; defensive options at Olm.
313.strategy: Survive the whole solo, especially Olm's final phase and Olm specials. Prioritise correct prayers, eating early, and safe positioning over speed; bring extra supplies.

314.setup: Ranged/magic to kill from distance; protect prayers and movement to dodge the acid/spit projectiles.
314.strategy: Avoid all shaman damage: dodge the green poison-spit projectiles (move off the tile they target) and use Protect from Missiles for their ranged attack while you finish a shaman. Solo or careful positioning makes avoiding splash damage easier.

315.stats: Any combat; puzzle-based, no real combat stat gate
315.setup: No special gear; this is the light-beam crystal puzzle. Just bring your raid kit.
315.strategy: Position the crabs/orbs so each orb after the first crystal aligns the beam correctly and damages a crab; plan the orb path before throwing so none miss. Use a known orb-order solution for the layout.

316.setup: Magic with fire spells (the demon must be finished with fire); Protect from Magic/Range as needed, stay mobile.
316.strategy: Light the brazier first, then avoid all the Ice Demon's ranged/magic attacks (use correct protection prayer and dodge the AoE ice) while DPSing. Take zero damage through the whole kill. Combine with the fire-spell finish for both tasks.

317.stats: 90+ combat all styles, 85+ Prayer, strong DPS
317.setup: High-DPS solo Olm gear (tbow, Sang/shadow, scythe/fang) plus overloads; needs clean mechanical execution.
317.strategy: Execute every Olm mechanic perfectly: dodge fire walls/crystal burst/bombs, never stand in a healing pool/teleport portal/prayer orb, keep the right (melee) claw dead so it cannot regen, and move between different acid pools so you never eat the same one twice in a row.

318.stats: 88+ combat all styles, 80+ Prayer, coordinated team
318.setup: Trio with strong DPS each (tbow/Sang/scythe); assigned roles for hands and head.
318.strategy: All three players must dodge every listed special and acid pools cleanly. Assign claw/head duties, call mechanics, and keep claws suppressed so they cannot regen. One mistake by any member fails it, so coordinate prayers and movement.

319.setup: Melee burst (scythe/fang) with DWH spec to drop Defence; rely on Tekton's predictable attack gaps.
319.strategy: Exploit Tekton's attack pattern: when he sparks/returns to the anvil he is not attacking, and his melee/lightning have telegraphs. DWH-spec then burst him down while only attacking during safe windows so he never lands a hit. Higher DPS shortens the no-hit window needed.

320.stats: 80+ Magic (fire spells), 70+ Defence/HP to tank ranged hits
320.setup: Magic with fire spells; high HP and decent Defence to absorb the unprotected ranged attacks, or dodge them.
320.strategy: Kill the Ice Demon while never turning on Protect from Range. Either tank the ranged hits with food or move to dodge its ranged attacks; you can still use Protect from Magic. Finish with fire to also satisfy the fire-spell task.

321.stats: 13+ Magic (Fire Strike) minimum; higher Magic for stronger fire spells
321.setup: Carry fire runes / a fire staff. Any Fire spell (Fire Strike upward) works for the finishing hit.
321.strategy: Bring it low with anything, then cast a Fire spell for the killing blow. The Ice Demon is immune to non-fire magic for the final blow context, so ensure your last hit is a Fire spell. Keep fire runes ready.
321.items: Fire spell runes (e.g. fire strike/bolt/blast/wave/surge); Staff or runes for a fire spell

322.stats: 90+ combat all styles, 90+ Magic, high DPS/supplies
322.setup: Speed-optimised solo: tbow, Sang/shadow, scythe/fang, DWH/BGS spec weapon; overloads, prayer enhances.
322.strategy: Pick a fast layout, skip non-essential content, use spec weapons (DWH on Tekton, etc.) and efficient prayers. Practise Olm to avoid slow phases. Sub-21 solo demands clean, fast room execution.

323.setup: BiS speedrun solo: twisted bow, shadow, scythe, spec weapons (DWH/BGS); overloads and prayer enhances throughout.
323.strategy: Requires highly optimised play: best layouts, perfect prayers, spec stacking, and fast Olm. Sub-17 solo is a serious speedrunning benchmark; minimise downtime and route efficiently.

324.stats: Team of high-level players; 90+ combat each, coordinated
324.setup: 5-man team with BiS gear and assigned roles; spec weapons (DWH/BGS), tbow/shadow/scythe across the team.
324.strategy: Coordinate role splitting (some rush rooms, DWH spammers on Tekton/Olm hands). Efficient 5-scale routing and high combined DPS get sub-15. Pre-assign who does what each room.

325.setup: 5 maxed players, BiS gear, multiple DWH/BGS for Defence drops, tbow/shadow/scythe DPS.
325.strategy: Tight speedrun: perfect role assignment, spec stacking on Tekton/Olm, and flawless room execution. Sub-12:30 5-scale is an organised-team time requiring practice as a group.

326.setup: 3-man BiS team with spec weapons (DWH/BGS) and full DPS switches; assigned hand/head roles at Olm.
326.strategy: Split rooms, stack DWH specs on Tekton and Olm, and run efficient prayers. Sub-16:30 trio needs a practised group and clean Olm.

327.setup: 3 maxed players, BiS gear, multiple spec weapons, optimal DPS; rehearsed roles.
327.strategy: Top-end trio speedrun: perfect routing, spec stacking, and fast Olm with assigned roles. Sub-14:30 requires a well-drilled team and minimal mistakes.

328.stats: 70+ Mining, 70+ Smithing, 70+ Runecraft (combat stats largely irrelevant)
328.setup: Best available pickaxe (Dragon/Crystal/Infernal), Graceful or Prospector for weight, Varrock armour for extra ore chance; food and stamina potions
328.strategy: Pure kill-count grind of 25 kills - join a public/team world for faster kills; combat level barely matters since damage comes from Mining/Smithing/Runecraft and avoiding the symbols/rocks.

329.setup: Best pickaxe, Graceful for run energy, stamina potions; minimal food needed if avoiding all damage cleanly
329.strategy: Best attempted solo (or with one trusted partner) so you control positioning. Step off shadow markers for falling rocks, stay 2+ tiles from glowing rocks when red energy balls fly in, move away when Zalcano starts to rise, and never stand on the orange/red damaging symbol (blue is safe and boosts damage). The 'no damage' run resets if you leave or get hit, so play slow and clean across all 5 kills.

330.setup: Best pickaxe and standard Zalcano setup; any weapon to quickly kill a golem (or just attack it unarmed)
330.strategy: During the fight, golems periodically spawn and walk toward Zalcano to restore her armour. Kill a golem before it reaches her - it drops 16-24 imbued tephra, completing 'Team Player'. This is easiest in a team where golems spawn frequently, or simply wait for a golem on a solo/duo kill.

331.stats: 70+ Mining, 70+ Smithing, 70+ Runecraft (higher is better for MVP)
331.setup: Best pickaxe (Dragon pickaxe +1 mining damage), high Smithing/Runecraft for stronger imbued tephra; stand on blue symbols for the 50% damage boost
331.strategy: Easiest done solo, where you are automatically the only/most-damage dealer. In a team, maximise output: throw imbued tephra while standing on the blue (damage-boosting) symbol during the armoured phase and mine aggressively with a Dragon pickaxe in the mining phase to out-damage everyone.

332.stats: 70+ Hitpoints (access), 80+ Attack/Strength, 80+ Defence, 75+ Magic, 43+ Prayer (70+ ideal)
332.setup: Melee: Bandos/Torva, demonbane weapon (Emberlight/Arclight) or crush weapon, Protect from Melee, occasional bursts of Protect from Magic. Bring a Zamorak item to avoid minion aggro.
332.strategy: Plain kill count to 50; wear a Zamorakian item (e.g. unholy book / Zamorak cape) so K'ril's followers ignore you, pray Melee and flick during his magic phases.

333.setup: Melee: high-tier gear (Bandos/Torva), Emberlight or Arclight, Protect from Melee, Zamorak item for minion aggro. Bank/return runs or instances to grind 100 kills efficiently.
333.strategy: Pure grind to 100 kills; identical method to the 50 KC task, just sustained longer, so bring efficient supplies (brews/restores or prayer potions).

334.setup: Melee with high DPS (Emberlight/Arclight or Bandos godsword) to burst him down, Protect from Melee.
334.strategy: The special only fires while you pray Protect from Melee, roughly 1-in-9 melee swings. Kill him fast enough (high DPS, ideally from low HP) that the special never triggers; if it does, abandon and reset.

335.setup: Melee with demonbane weapon (Emberlight/Arclight) plus a way to clear minions; a private instance makes timing easier. Do NOT wear a Zamorak item so minions stay aggressive/killable, or just kill them directly.
335.strategy: Kill all three minions first, then finish K'ril before any of them respawn. Best done in an instance: clear Balfrug, Tstanon and Zakl'n, then burst K'ril while they are all down.

336.stats: 62/72/82 Magic for Inferior/Superior/Dark Demonbane respectively, 70+ Hitpoints, 80+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 75 Arceuus favour
336.setup: Arceuus spellbook, best Magic gear (Ancestral/occult), cast Mark of Darkness then Dark Demonbane; Purging staff/Emberlight passive boosts demonbane spell damage. Tank with high Magic defence and food.
336.strategy: Must be a private instance. Cast Mark of Darkness for the damage boost, then attack only with the demonbane spell line (Dark Demonbane is strongest). Avoid auto-retaliating with melee/range, which would void the task.
336.items: Inferior Demonbane (spell); Superior Demonbane (spell); Dark Demonbane (spell); Arceuus spellbook access; Mark of Darkness (recommended); Purging staff (optional, boosts demonbane spells); Water runes; Soul runes; Required combination runes for demonbane spells

337.setup: Ranged or Magic from outside his melee range, OR full melee with Protect from Melee active the entire fight. High Magic defence gear to survive his mage attacks.
337.strategy: Easiest: stay out of melee range and attack with Magic (water spells, he's weak to water) or Ranged so he can only use his magic attack. If meleeing, keep Protect from Melee up continuously and never let a single melee hit land; do it in an instance for safety.

338.setup: Wear a Zamorakian item (Zamorak cape/unholy book/staff) so all three followers stay unaggressive, then fight K'ril normally with melee + Protect from Melee in an instance.
338.strategy: Equip any Zamorak-aligned item so the bodyguards never attack you, then simply kill K'ril without ever stepping out of that protection or dropping the item. The bodyguards never landing a hit is the entire requirement.

339.stats: 70+ Hitpoints, 75+ Attack/Strength (for melee demonbane), or 75+ Ranged for Scorching bow, 80+ Defence, 43+ Prayer
339.setup: Any demonbane weapon equipped for the kill: melee (Arclight/Emberlight/Darklight/Silverlight/Burning claws) or ranged (Scorching bow). Arclight/Emberlight are the strongest and ideal here.
339.strategy: Only the finishing hit must come from a demonbane weapon, so you can fight with anything then swap to e.g. Arclight/Emberlight to land the kill blow.
339.items: Silverlight; Darklight; Arclight; Emberlight; Burning claws; Scorching bow; Purging staff

340.setup: Melee with high DPS (Emberlight/Arclight, Bandos/Torva), Protect from Melee, Zamorak item for minion aggro. Inventory must hold enough supplies for all 20 kills: Saradomin brews + Super restores + prayer potions, plus high-HP food.
340.strategy: Stamina/endurance task: bring max sustain (brews/restores) since you cannot bank between the 20 kills. Use overhead Protect from Melee and efficient prayer flicking to make supplies last; a Zamorak item keeps minions off you.

341.setup: Ranged main style (Twisted bow or blowpipe) with Rigour; switch to mage prayer/style vs Jal-Zek. Full void/crystal/masori, anguish, pegasians.
341.strategy: Jal-Zek (the magic attacker) first appears on wave 9 and on most later waves, so you do not need a full run - just survive deep enough into a wave to kill one. Pray Mage when it attacks and burst it down; it can revive itself if a Jal-Xil is alive, so kill the Jal-Zek last or finish it cleanly.
341.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Toxic blowpipe; Armadyl crossbow

342.setup: Best-in-slot ranged: Twisted bow + Masori (f), Necklace of anguish, Pegasian boots, Rigour; Saradomin brews + super restores; mage switch for Jal-Zek.
342.strategy: Five full Inferno completions - this is a consistency grind, not a single-run challenge. Use a reliable, repeatable strategy (Twisted bow + brews) and prioritise survival over speed on each run.
342.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow; Toxic blowpipe

343.setup: Twisted bow + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; keep a melee weapon (e.g. Ghrazi rapier/blowpipe) handy to finish the Jal-ImKot fast.
343.strategy: Jal-ImKot (the melee blob) burrows after 30s if it cannot reach you. Never safespot it away - always stay in melee range or kill it immediately each wave so its dig timer never elapses. The timer resets if it lands at least one hit, so let it attack before repositioning.
343.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

344.setup: High-DPS ranged: Twisted bow (or zaryte crossbow) + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; brews/restores for the triple-Jad damage rush.
344.strategy: Wave 68 has three JalTok-Jads attacking 3 ticks apart, each summoning Yt-HurKot healers at half health. To beat the 30s timer after the first Jad dies, burst the other two down before their healers fully heal them - off-pray/ignore the healers and dump max DPS, flicking prayers for the staggered attacks.
344.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

345.setup: Twisted bow + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians; Rigour + perfect prayer flicking; brews/restores in case of nibbler/other chip damage.
345.strategy: Stay behind the moving shield so Zuk never lands a hit, and prayer-flick Jad attacks perfectly - a Jad deals 0 if you pray correctly on its attack tick. Other enemies (nibblers, mages) are allowed to hit you; only Zuk's hits and Jad damage break this task.
345.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

346.setup: Twisted bow + crystal/Masori, anguish, pegasians, Rigour; keep a fast weapon (blowpipe) to clear nibblers each wave.
346.strategy: Each pillar has 255 HP and nibblers (Jal-Nib, max hit 4 crush) chew them down every wave. Kill nibblers ASAP each wave so the three pillars survive until wave 67, when the game auto-destroys them anyway. Prioritise nibblers over other spawns early.
346.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

347.setup: Defensive max ranged: Twisted bow + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; load extra Saradomin brews and stay high HP throughout.
347.strategy: Never let HP dip under 50, so brew up early and aggressively - eat/brew before big hits rather than after. Watch the wave-69 JalTok-Jad and Zuk's 148 max hit; sip brews proactively and keep prayer flicking flawless to avoid spike damage.
347.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

348.setup: Non-Tbow ranged: Zaryte/Armadyl crossbow with ruby/diamond dragon bolts (e) and Toxic blowpipe for fast spawns; Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour.
348.strategy: Simply do the whole run without ever wielding a Twisted bow. A blowpipe (fast spawns) plus a crossbow with ruby/diamond enchanted bolts for Jad/Zuk burst is the standard 'budget' loadout; everything else plays as a normal Inferno.
348.items: Fire cape; Armadyl crossbow; Toxic blowpipe; Zaryte crossbow

349.setup: Pure ranged: Twisted bow + crossbow/blowpipe, Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; no spellbook attacks - bring extra ammo instead of relying on barrage.
349.strategy: Do not cast any spell - kill the magic-attacking Jal-Zek and all spawns with ranged only, and avoid using a powered staff/standard spells. Use Protect from Magic and ranged DPS to handle Jal-Zek instead of mage retaliation.
349.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow; Toxic blowpipe

350.setup: Melee for waves 1-68: Ghrazi rapier/Inquisitor's or Scythe, full Justiciar/Torva, Avernic; swap to ranged (Twisted bow) only once Zuk appears on wave 69.
350.strategy: This is a melee-only Inferno until Zuk: you must walk up to every spawn (including ranged Jal-Xil and mage Jal-Zek) and kill them with melee while flicking overhead prayers. Extremely punishing positioning; only equip a ranged/mage weapon after wave 69 begins to kill Zuk.
350.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow; Ghrazi rapier; Toxic blowpipe

351.setup: Twisted bow + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; prayer for both Zuk's set and the Jad while ignoring the Jad's HP.
351.strategy: When Zuk drops below 480 HP a JalTok-Jad spawns - leave it alive and finish Zuk while tanking/praying against the Jad. Flick Protect prayers against the Jad's attacks but never deal the killing blow to it; race Zuk down instead.
351.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow

352.setup: High-DPS ranged: Twisted bow + Masori (f), anguish, pegasians, Rigour; blowpipe for fast spawn clears; mage switch for Jal-Zek.
352.strategy: Average runs run long, so push DPS and minimise downtime: stack spawns to hit multiple enemies with Twisted bow, clear nibblers fast, and avoid resetting/safespotting that wastes ticks. Efficient prayer flicking lets you stay aggressive instead of stalling for safety.
352.items: Fire cape; Twisted bow; Armadyl crossbow; Toxic blowpipe

353.setup: Harpoon/barbarian rod for fishing, hammer to repair, bucket to douse fires, rope to tether; angler's outfit optional for XP.
353.strategy: Pure Fishing activity with no combat requirement; just subdue Tempoross 5 times. Group fishing the spirit pool in the essence phase is the fastest way to rack up subdues.

354.setup: Harpoon/barbarian rod, hammer, bucket, rope; angler's outfit optional for XP.
354.strategy: Same as the Novice task, just 10 subdues. No combat involved; focus on harpoonfish and the spirit pool to deplete essence quickly.

355.stats: 35+ Fishing; angler's outfit obtained from Fishing Trawler/aerial fishing
355.setup: Full standard Angler's outfit OR full Spirit angler's outfit (hat, top, waders, boots) worn for the whole subdue; plus harpoon, hammer, bucket, rope.
355.strategy: Equip all four angler pieces (standard or spirit variation both qualify) before the final hit so you are wearing the set at the moment of subdue. The outfit also gives a small Fishing XP bonus.
355.items: Angler hat; Angler top; Angler waders; Angler boots; Harpoon; Hammer; Rope; Bucket

356.setup: Bucket of water (fill from the water pump/dock) to douse fires; standard harpoon, hammer, rope kit.
356.strategy: Lightning attacks set fires on the docks; fill a bucket at the pump and click each fire to put it out. Doing it solo guarantees you personally extinguish 5 since no one else competes for them.

357.setup: Hammer to repair the broken mast/totem pole; standard harpoon, bucket, rope kit.
357.strategy: When a wave or torrent damages a mast or totem pole, click it with a hammer in your inventory to repair it. Easiest done solo so the broken structure is still there when you reach it.

358.stats: High Fishing (76+ for double harpoonfish) strongly recommended to hit the point threshold
358.setup: Dragon/infernal harpoon for faster catches, fish barrel and spirit flakes to boost catch rate, full angler's outfit; hammer, bucket, rope.
358.strategy: Maximise personal points: fill cannons, douse fires, tether totems, repair masts and fish heavily. Solo or a small group with a long fight (3 cycles) makes ~8,300 points achievable. Spirit flakes and a fish barrel raise throughput.

359.stats: 35+ Fishing; mechanical awareness more important than stats
359.setup: Standard harpoon, hammer, bucket, rope; no special gear needed, just careful play.
359.strategy: Solo only: stay off burning tiles, tether to a totem/mast before each wave so it cannot push/hit you, and move away from cannons during torrents to avoid the stun. Avoiding all three damage sources for the whole fight is the challenge.

360.setup: Harpoonfish/ammunition crates to load cannons; standard harpoon, hammer, bucket, rope.
360.strategy: Each ship has two cannons; carry cooked/raw harpoonfish to a cannon, load it and fire, then do the same for the other three. Best done solo so no one else claims the cannons before you load all four.

361.stats: 85+ Ranged, 70+ Defence, 44+ Prayer (43 min for Protect prayers + Eagle Eye)
361.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow or Toxic blowpipe, Armadyl/Masori with prayer-switch flicking between Protect from Magic and Protect from Missiles
361.strategy: Two JalTok-Jads (cl 900) attack simultaneously; flick the correct overhead the instant each fires its ranged/magic attack, and burst both down with a Twisted bow before stacked hits overwhelm you.
361.items: Saradomin brew; Super restore; Ranging potion (or Bastion/Divine ranging potion)

362.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with Armadyl/Masori, Rigour, flicking Protect from Magic/Missiles; brews and restores for sustain
362.strategy: Four Jads fire together, so a single missed prayer flick can chunk you for up to 113; keep Saradomin brew HP buffer high and prioritise killing Jads to thin the simultaneous attacks. Requires Inferno completion to access.
362.items: Saradomin brew; Super restore; Ranging potion (or Bastion/Divine ranging potion)

363.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with Masori/Armadyl, Rigour, rapid prayer flicking; full brew/restore inventory
363.strategy: Six Jads attack at once, demanding flawless pray-switching and constant Saradomin brew topping; tank the first attacks behind the correct overhead then burn Jads down quickly to reduce incoming volleys. Requires Inferno completion.
363.items: Saradomin brew; Super restore; Ranging potion (or Bastion/Divine ranging potion)

364.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with max ranged gear (Masori/Armadyl, Rigour) for highest DPS against a single Jad
364.strategy: Only one Jad, so bring maximum DPS (Twisted bow ideal) and a Ranging/Bastion potion; flick prayers correctly while dealing full damage to drop it well within 45 seconds.
364.items: Twisted bow (or strong DPS weapon); Ranging potion; Saradomin brew

365.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with max ranged gear and Rigour for fast kills on all three Jads
365.strategy: Three minutes is generous, so prioritise survival with prayer flicks while still pushing solid DPS; kill Jads one at a time to reduce simultaneous attacks. Requires Inferno completion to access challenge 3.
365.items: Twisted bow (or strong DPS weapon); Ranging potion; Saradomin brew; Super restore

366.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with full max ranged gear and Rigour to clear five Jads within the time limit
366.strategy: Five Jads with a 5-minute cap means you must keep up DPS while flawlessly flicking overheads; thin the pack quickly to lower incoming damage and avoid wasting time healing. Requires Inferno completion.
366.items: Twisted bow (or strong DPS weapon); Ranging potion; Saradomin brew; Super restore

367.setup: Melee: Inquisitor's/Torva or Bandos with Ghrazi rapier or Inquisitor's mace, Avernic defender, Piety, flicking Protect from Magic/Missiles
367.strategy: Two Jads with melee only means you must stand in range of both and flick the correct overhead while attacking; since you cannot pick targets at distance, manage positioning and brew HP high to survive missed flicks.
367.items: Inquisitor's mace (or Ghrazi rapier/Osmumten's fang); Avernic defender; Saradomin brew; Super restore; Super combat potion

368.setup: Melee: Inquisitor's/Torva with Ghrazi rapier or Inquisitor's mace, Avernic defender, Piety, constant overhead flicking
368.strategy: Four Jads melee only is highly punishing - you can only hit Jads adjacent to you, so kill them one at a time while flicking Protect from Magic/Missiles and keeping a large Saradomin brew HP buffer. Requires Inferno completion.
368.items: Inquisitor's mace (or Ghrazi rapier/Osmumten's fang); Avernic defender; Saradomin brew; Super restore; Super combat potion

369.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with max ranged gear; no inventory items, so all sustain comes from worn gear only
369.strategy: With nothing to heal, this is pure prayer-flicking - never take an unprotected hit from any of the three Jads; flick overheads perfectly and lean on max DPS to end the fight before any chip damage builds. Requires Inferno completion.

370.setup: Ranged: Twisted bow with full max ranged gear and Rigour; bring ample brews/restores for both consecutive challenges
370.strategy: You must win challenge 5 (5 Jads) then immediately challenge 6 (6 Jads) without a death/failure between; maintain perfect prayer flicks across both and conserve supplies so you arrive at challenge 6 well-stocked. Requires Inferno completion.
370.items: Twisted bow (or strong DPS weapon); Ranging potion; Saradomin brew; Super restore

371.setup: Hybrid: carry a ranged weapon, a magic weapon, and a melee weapon plus matching offensive prayer switches; kill one Jad with each style
371.strategy: Three Jads, each must die to a distinct style - assign one Jad to melee, one to ranged, one to magic and swap weapons/offensive prayers between kills while still flicking defensive overheads. Plan a clean weapon-swap order to avoid finishing two Jads with the same style. Requires Inferno completion.
371.items: Twisted bow or ranged weapon; Powered staff or spell (e.g. Tumeken's shadow/Sang/Trident); Melee weapon (e.g. Ghrazi rapier); Saradomin brew; Super restore

372.stats: 99 Hitpoints, 90+ in main attack stat, 77+ Prayer, 90+ Defence
372.setup: Max melee (Scythe of vitur) plus ranged for spawns; coordinated team DPS
372.strategy: Maiden drops blood spawns at 70%/50%/30% (N1,N2,N3); each spawn that survives long enough leaves a blood trail. Burst her phases fast with Scythe and kill/ignore Matomenos efficiently so fewer than 16 trails are ever created across the fight.

376.setup: Max melee (Scythe of vitur) for the screech/stare phase; defensive armour

378.strategy: In Verzik P3 the yellow ball/green-blast hits all players; while tornadoes are active and 2+ players are alive, every player must position to dodge the blast (avoid the targeted tile/spread correctly) so none take damage. Keep at least 2 alive when it fires.

379.setup: Ranged (Twisted bow/Blowpipe) and/or magic (Shadow) for P3; no melee weapon equipped vs Verzik in P3

384.stats: Maxed combat (99s), 77+ Prayer, 99 Hitpoints, high DPS gear

385.setup: Best-in-slot per style: Scythe, Twisted bow/Blowpipe, Shadow

386.stats: 80+ Ranged or Magic, 70+ Defence, 44+ Prayer, 80+ HP
386.setup: Ranged (rune crossbow / blowpipe / msb(i)) or magic for fast bloodspawn kills; void or budget ranged gear, slayer/diary food

Coordinate so no single spawn is left alive too long; a small team or solo makes timing easier to control.

387.setup: Salve amulet (ei) equipped (replaces glory/anguish/torture); ranged or melee Bloat gear, stamina potions
387.strategy: Salve amulet boosts damage vs Bloat (undead). Everyone simply equips a salve amulet for the whole Bloat fight - swap your usual neck slot for it before starting. Bring stamina potions to manage movement around the falling limbs.

388.setup: Standard Bloat gear; no special items needed - this is a positioning task
388.strategy: After Maiden, run around to the far entrance and enter the Bloat room from the opposite side of the door before the fight begins. Purely a movement/positioning achievement; just take the long way into the room.

389.stats: 75+ Attack/Strength, 44+ Prayer, 70+ HP, enough spec energy
389.setup: Crystal halberd (preferred, hits twice) or Dragon halberd in melee gear; ensure ~30%+ special energy when Bloat is low
389.strategy: Bring a halberd and full special-attack energy. DPS Bloat down normally, then when it is in its walking/stunned phase and at very low HP, use the halberd special attack as the final attack to score the kill. Crystal halberd's double-hit spec makes the timing more forgiving.
389.items: Crystal halberd; Dragon halberd; Crawling Hand (n/a)

390.stats: 94 Magic (Ice Barrage), 75+ Defence, Ancient Magicks unlocked
390.setup: Ancient Magicks on Ice Barrage; rune pouch with death/water/blood runes; mage gear (ahrim's/occult/trident not needed for freeze)
390.strategy: During the Nylocas wave, wait until at least 4 Nylocas are bunched together (Ice Barrage is a 3x3 AoE freeze) then cast a single Ice Barrage on the cluster. Requires Ancient Magicks; group/duo runs make stacking 4 in one square cluster easier.
390.items: Rune pouch; Death rune; Water rune; Blood rune; Ancient Magicks (Ice Barrage)

391.setup: Ranged or magic Sotetseg gear; team of 2+ required
391.strategy: When Sotetseg launches the large red ball (every ~10 attacks), the whole team must stack on the same tile so the damage is split among everyone. Do this for the ball during the fight and finish the kill. Must be in a group of 2 or more - cannot be done solo.

392.setup: Ranged or magic Xarpus gear; avoid over-DPS during the reflect phase
392.strategy: In the final phase Xarpus turns his head and reflects a percentage of damage dealt while he is facing/staring. Only attack him while he is looking away; stop all attacks the moment he turns to face the player so no reflected damage is taken. Coordinate the team to hold DPS during the stare.

393.setup: Dawnbringer (given in-instance) to break P1 shield; ranged gear, hide behind pillars
393.strategy: Verzik's P1 acid/magic attack destroys a pillar if a player is hiding behind it when hit. Use the Dawnbringer special to drop her shield quickly and spread out / move between pillars so no single pillar absorbs enough hits to collapse. Kill P1 fast before pillars are worn down.

394.setup: Ranged gear (blowpipe / msb(i) / crossbow); tick-perfect walking
394.strategy: In P2 Verzik melees (bounces) the nearest player. Use tick-perfect 4- or 5-tick walking (attack, step, wait) to stay out of melee range so she never connects a bounce on anyone the whole phase. Keep moving and never stand adjacent when she is about to attack.

395.setup: Ranged Verzik gear; team of 2+ to receive the ball
395.strategy: In Verzik P3 a green ball targets a player and grows; running adjacent to a teammate passes it to them (it must be bounced between players to be defused). When the ball is on the player, run next to a teammate to hand it off - completing one successful pass satisfies the task. Needs at least one other player.

396.stats: 85+ Ranged/Magic, 80+ Defence, 77+ Magic (Ice spells helpful), 44+ Prayer, 85+ HP
396.setup: Strong ranged + mage switches, salve/slayer boosts, brews/restores; efficient duo or trio with good gear
396.strategy: Pace every room - fast bloodspawn handling at Maiden, quick Bloat, efficient Nylocas colour-killing, stacked Sotetseg balls, and clean Verzik phases. A geared duo/trio with style switches clears well under 17 min; minimise deaths and downtime between rooms.

397.stats: 70+ Ranged/Magic, 70+ Defence, 44+ Prayer, 70+ HP; Ice spells (combo eq.) optional but helpful
397.setup: Ranged/magic gear with melee for grey Nylocas; brews, restores, stamina potions
397.strategy: Just complete an Entry Mode run once. Entry Mode is the forgiving version - bring food/brews, learn each room's mechanic, and a team or duo can carry inexperienced players. Plain completion, no restriction.

398.setup: Any decent melee weapon (e.g. rune scimitar / better) with adamant+ armour, or a ranged setup; food and Protect from Melee for the Fire Giants. A dwarf multicannon may be used in the den.
398.strategy: All three giants live together in the Giants' Den (the Shayzien 'giant cave', south-west of the Giant Pit). Just kill one Hill Giant (lvl 28), one Moss Giant (lvl 42) and one Fire Giant (lvl 104/109) in this one dungeon to complete it. Reach it via fairy ring DJR, Kourend Castle/Xeric's teleport, or the Lovakengj minecart to Shayzien West.

399.stats: 38+ Magic (Lesser thralls), 25+ Prayer for sustain; A Kingdom Divided quest complete. Higher Magic (57/76) for Superior/Greater thralls = faster.
399.setup: Arceuus spellbook with Book of the Dead equipped; cast a thrall spell (Ghostly/Skeletal/Zombified) on any monster you are fighting. No combat gear matters since the thrall does the damage.
399.strategy: Summon a thrall (any tier) before/while fighting any low-level monster and let it auto-attack; thralls always hit, so just rack up 100 total thrall damage. Lesser thrall (max 1) works but is slow; Superior (max 2) or Greater (max 3) finish it much faster. Re-summon when each thrall expires.
399.items: Book of the Dead; Arceuus spellbook access; Runes for a thrall spell (e.g. Lesser: 10 Air, 1 Cosmic, 5 Mind; Superior: 10 Earth, 1 Cosmic, 5 Death; Greater: 10 Fire, 5 Blood, 1 Cosmic)

402.setup: Crush melee BiS (Scythe/Inquisitor's mace + full Inquisitor's), Piety, max supplies

402.items: Crush weapon (Inquisitor's mace or Scythe of vitur); Saradomin brew; Super restore / Prayer potion; Sanfew serum

403.strategy: Perfect run: pray the right protection vs every attack (only husks, power blasts, and weakened parasites are 'allowed' damage), never stand within 1 tile of a Nightmare Spore (avoids the attack-slow/yawn), and kill every sleepwalker before it reaches her. Bringing high crush DPS makes sleepwalker/parasite kills reliable.

404.strategy: Speed task — maximise damage uptime: don't over-pray-switch into DPS loss, clear totems fast, and avoid spore slow which wastes attacks. 7:30 is lenient and achievable on most legit kills.

406.stats: 99 Strength with high strength bonus (approx 118 effective Strength / 160+ str bonus), Piety active

407.setup: Standard crush BiS/Bandos with Piety; pay attention to prayer-flicking in phase 3

408.stats: 90+ melee combat, 70+ Prayer; high DPS to clear sleepwalkers
408.setup: High-DPS crush (Scythe/Inquisitor's mace) plus a quick switch (e.g. ranged) to one-shot sleepwalkers as they spawn

415.setup: Standard ranged setup; positioning matters more than gear for this task.
415.strategy: Mechanical: in the Smoke phase Nex darts down one of the four aisles hitting everyone in her path for up to 50. Watch the directional cue and step out of the aisle she charges down so no one is in the line.

418.setup: Standard ranged/hybrid; deliberately avoid gas protection (no gas mask/facemask/slayer helm) so the cough lasts longer.
418.strategy: Restriction: a player must be coughing when Nex dies. Get infected by the Smoke phase Choke (the virus spreads between adjacent players), avoid gas protection so it persists, then finish the kill while still afflicted. Coordinate timing on the final phase.
418.items: Frozen key; Zaryte crossbow; Masori armour; Saradomin brews; Super restores

419.setup: Full hybrid with a stab/crush weapon or salamander for ice prisons; coordinated experienced team, thralls for DPS.
419.strategy: Perfection: combine the four mechanical tasks in a single kill. Dodge the Smoke Dash aisle, move off Shadow Smash within 3 ticks, stop attacking the kneeling Nex during Blood Siphon (sacrifice runs away), and break/avoid all ice prisons and Containment. Requires a tight, practiced team.
419.items: Frozen key; Zaryte crossbow; Masori armour; Osmumten's fang; Elder maul; Swamp lizard; Saradomin brews; Super restores

420.setup: Standard ranged setup; movement/positioning is the key, not gear.
420.strategy: Mechanical: in the Shadow phase deadly shadows appear under every player and erupt after 3 ticks for up to 50. Move off your tile within those 3 ticks so no one is hit.
420.items: Frozen key; Zaryte crossbow; Masori armour; Saradomin brews; Super restores

421.stats: 90+ Ranged, 90+ Magic, 80+ Melee stats, 77+ Prayer (Rigour/Augury ideal), 70+ Defence
421.setup: Tbow or Bowfa + crystal/masori for ranged DPS, Shadow/Sang if available for magic, Fang for melee; Salve (ei) where applicable, Prayer/Stamina potions, brews/restores
421.strategy: Run a low raid level (e.g. 150 path or even base) for fastest, safest clears; bring strong DPS and skip optional sarcophagi/loot, rush each room to beat the 18-minute timer.

422.setup: Best-in-slot tri-brid: ranged (Masori/Twisted bow or Zaryte cbow), magic (ancestral/shadow), melee (Torva + fang/scythe); Bandos for prayer flicking.
422.strategy: Run efficient invocations for a low raid level (just above 300) to keep boss HP/mechanics low; prioritise fast boss kills over puzzle perfection. Solo or small organised team is most consistent for the 20-min cap.

423.setup: Coordinated 8-man team in BiS tri-brid gear; assign roles (DPS, prayer/teleport) per boss.
423.strategy: 8-scale bosses have far more HP, so this needs a practised group with high collective DPS and clean mechanics; minimise puzzle/run time and avoid downs. Harder than the solo 20-min version.

424.setup: Best owned weapons by style (Tbow/Bowfa, Shadow/Sang, Fang) since the gear restriction is on consumables only, not equipment
424.strategy: You may still bring full gear; only consumables must come from inside the raid, so play safe rooms, conserve the spirit's supplies, and avoid all avoidable damage to prevent running dry and dying.

425.setup: Strong style-appropriate gear; pack generous own brews/restores, Saradomin brews, ranging/super combat pots and food
425.strategy: Bring plenty of your own supplies and simply never loot the Helpful Spirit chest or honey locusts; run a comfortable raid level and avoid avoidable damage to guarantee no deaths.

426.stats: 90+ offensive stats, 77+ Prayer, 90+ Defence, high Hitpoints
426.setup: Defensive/tanky tri-brid with strong prayer; bring Saradomin brews are blocked so rely on Prayer-flicking and damage avoidance.
426.strategy: With no food and no brews, survival depends entirely on prayer flicking and avoiding all mechanics; play very safe, keep raid level modest, and never let anyone go below ~50% HP. Blood spells/soul-split (n/a) not available, so play conservatively.

427.setup: Sub-75 gear only: e.g. blessed d'hide + rune/dragon crossbow, mystic/ahrim's (check tier), whip/dragon scimitar, fighter torso, void as a strong option (void has a 42 req so allowed).
427.strategy: Lower the raid level to ease the DPS loss from weaker gear; Void Knight kit and sub-75 BiS (e.g. Rune crossbow, ancient staff, Abyssal whip) keeps you functional. Verify each item's level requirement is under 75 before entering.

428.setup: Osmumten's Fang (or Ghrazi rapier/whip) with melee armour (Bandos/Torva, Salve where useful), Protect from Magic, brews/restores
428.strategy: Melee Zebak from under/beside him; pray Magic against his basic attack, manage jugs and blood/wave mechanics manually, and only ever use melee weapons to keep the restriction valid.
428.items: Fang; Osmumten's fang; Ghrazi rapier; Zamorakian hasta; Abyssal whip

429.setup: Melee: Torva, Scythe of Vitur or Osmumten's fang, Avernic defender, Amulet of torture/rancour, Prayer for Piety.
429.strategy: Insanity makes p3 slams faster, so pre-position for the slam/skull mechanic; only melee the Warden core. Being in a group means others can DPS too, but YOU must survive and use only melee. Prayer-flick the obelisk/slam damage.

430.setup: Solid style gear per role; everyone brings ample brews/restores and protection prayers
430.strategy: Run a low/comfortable raid level with a coordinated team; assign roles, communicate boss mechanics, and prioritise survival over speed so nobody dies.

431.setup: Strong all-style switches (Tbow/Bowfa, Shadow/Sang, Fang), full prayer support, generous brews/restores
431.strategy: Solo a low raid level you are comfortable with; learn each boss's safe patterns and play defensively, topping up HP/prayer between rooms to avoid any death.

432.setup: High-DPS ranged/magic (Tbow/Bowfa or Shadow) to break the core in one exposure
432.strategy: Stand in the beam's safe spot, avoid the orbs entirely, and burst the exposed core down before it covers again so you only need one exposure with zero beam/orb damage.

433.setup: Full style switches to match Akkha's prayer (Tbow/Bowfa, Shadow/Sang, Fang) plus correct overhead prayers and protection prayers
433.strategy: Activate every Akkha invocation, swap to the style Akkha is NOT praying and the matching overhead, clear shadows before they detonate, position for the memory/detonate/orb specials, and dodge enrage orbs to keep a flawless run.

434.setup: Akkha: switch ranged/magic to match his style swaps; prayer switches for his attack styles. BiS tri-brid.
434.strategy: Het: don't get hit by the warden statue's light beams while breaking the core. Akkha: prayer-swap correctly every attack, dodge trailing orbs and the detonate (memory) shadow explosions, and avoid his quadrant specials. One mistake in either room fails the GM combo.

435.stats: 70+ Ranged/Magic, 60+ Agility for the room hazards
435.setup: Standard raid gear; ranged/magic to clear baboons quickly
435.strategy: Coordinate the team to clear baboons fast, avoid stepping in venom and the danger tiles, kill volatile baboons before they reach you, and keep moving to finish under 3 minutes.

436.setup: Fang/melee for Ba-Ba plus ranged switch; Protect from Melee, brews/restores
436.strategy: Enable all Ba-Ba invocations, pray Melee against her slam, dodge rolling boulders and rubble/falling-rock tiles, and never open a sarcophagus so the run stays flawless.

437.setup: Ba-Ba: melee (Scythe/Fang) is standard; bring ranged for baboons. Tri-brid BiS.
437.strategy: Apmeken: in the venom/baboon rooms avoid contact damage and the volatile (exploding) baboons. Ba-Ba: stand correctly to dodge boulders, step away from rolling rubble and the knockback slams at 66%/33% (never fall in the pit), and kill the boulder/rubble safely.

438.stats: Combat level mainly for raid access; agility/movement focus
438.setup: Light gear with Stamina potion for fast running
438.strategy: Fill your water container, take the most direct route, watch for spillage tiles, and block/avoid crocodiles so none reach the tree, all within the 1-minute timer.

439.setup: Ranged/magic DPS (Tbow/Bowfa, Shadow) with Protect from Magic and brews/restores
439.strategy: Activate every Zebak invocation, pray Magic against his basic, dodge waves/blood, avoid poison pools, and on the roar push at most two jugs (destroy the rest) to satisfy the flawless conditions.

440.setup: Zebak: ranged or magic from distance; BiS tri-brid. Crondis puzzle needs no combat gear.
440.strategy: Crondis: navigate the spike traps/poison without taking hits while filling the waterskins and growing the palm. Zebak: pray correctly, push the jug to block the tidal wave/roar, dodge acid pools and blood clouds, and avoid the wave damage entirely.

441.stats: Combat level mainly for raid access; puzzle-solving focus
441.setup: Standard raid gear; Stamina potion for speed
441.strategy: Solve the matching/light puzzles quickly and accurately, stepping only on safe tiles, to finish within 1 minute without taking any puzzle damage.

442.setup: Ranged/magic DPS plus chinchompas/AoE for scarab and egg control; Protect from Missiles
442.strategy: Clear all eggs (with AoE/chins) before any hatch, pray Missiles against Kephri, manage bodyguards and exploding scarabs, and dodge dung tiles to keep the fight flawless.

443.setup: Kephri: ranged (chinchompas for scarab swarms / Twisted bow on Kephri); bring a way to clear scarabs and dung. BiS tri-brid.
443.strategy: Scabaras: solve the obelisk/tile/symbol puzzles correctly (avoid the random pillar room solo). Kephri: chin the scarab swarms, kill bodyguards during shield phases, dodge the 3x3 aerial bombs and dung strikes, and don't let her heal back.

444.setup: Full style switches (Tbow/Bowfa, Shadow/Sang, Fang), correct overhead prayers, brews/restores
444.strategy: Enable all Wardens invocations, dodge obelisk and P1/P2 patterns, and in P3 avoid lightning, the skull attack and demi-god hits while keeping prayers correct to stay flawless.

445.setup: Full tri-brid BiS; phase-appropriate styles (ranged/mage on cores, melee where applicable).
445.strategy: Avoid all avoidable damage across p1 (charge/obelisk), p2 (energy siphon, obelisk attacks, lightning) and p3 (slams, skulls). With all Wardens invocations active the windows are tight; assign obelisks and pre-pray the slam. Lowest completion % perfection in the file (1.5%).

446.setup: BiS tri-brid; defensive prayers and high-HP setup for the harder mechanics at RL500.
446.strategy: Stack invocations to reach 500+ (boss invocations, path levels, mechanical ones like Insanity/Walk the Path). Higher raid level means more boss HP and harder mechanics, so play very safe; one death anywhere fails it.

447.setup: BiS or strong mid-game tri-brid; defensive prayers.
447.strategy: Expert mode (300+) already clears most of this; add a few invocations to push past RL350. Focus on clean mechanics and no deaths. Highest completion % in the file (13.7%) - an accessible expert milestone.

448.setup: Full BiS tri-brid; maximise DPS to offset the inflated boss HP, plus tanky switches.
448.strategy: All boss invocations stack a huge amount of boss HP and every boss's hard mechanic at once; needs strong DPS and flawless prayer/mechanic play to avoid any death. One of the hardest GM tasks here (1.6% completion).

449.setup: Style switches to match Akkha's prayer plus protection prayers; brews/restores
449.strategy: Pick up/contain every materialising orb so none are dropped (avoid the off-prayer/quadrant orb mechanic), while swapping styles correctly and surviving the fight.

450.setup: Akkha: ranged + magic to match his style swaps; prayer switches; BiS tri-brid.
450.strategy: Path level 4 boosts Akkha's HP/mechanics; activate all his invocations (e.g. Insanity-tier Akkha mods). Prayer-swap each phase, dodge the trailing orbs and detonate/memory shadow explosions, and clear his quadrant specials. You only need to survive yourself.

451.setup: Fang/melee with Protect from Melee; Boulderdash invocation enabled
451.strategy: Turn on Boulderdash and, during the boulder phase, only hit the full (non-weakened) boulders to break them, never the weakened ones, while praying Melee and avoiding boulder hits.

452.setup: Fang/melee DPS with Protect from Melee; brews/restores
452.strategy: Burst Ba-Ba's health down fast so she enters fewer boulder phases, destroying at most four boulders total across the whole fight while surviving.

453.setup: Ba-Ba: melee (Scythe/Fang), ranged for boulders/baboons; BiS or strong mid-game.
453.strategy: Path 4 + all Ba-Ba invocations increase boulder/slam difficulty. Dodge rolling boulders and rubble, step clear of the 66%/33% knockback slams (never fall in the pit), and kill the boulder safely. Survive yourself only.

454.setup: Zebak: ranged/magic from distance; do NOT use any prayer (or only prayers that don't drain... in practice keep prayer untouched). BiS tri-brid.
454.strategy: You must not spend any prayer points, so fight Zebak with prayer off entirely and tank/dodge everything manually while still meeting Rockin' Around the Croc (all Zebak invocations, path 4, no death). Avoid acid, blood clouds, push the jug to block the wave, and out-position the roar.

455.setup: Zebak: ranged/magic from distance; protect from magic/missiles as needed. BiS tri-brid.
455.strategy: Path 4 + all Zebak invocations raise his HP and mechanic intensity. Push the jug to block the tidal wave/roar, avoid acid pools and blood clouds, and pray against his attacks. Survive yourself only.

456.setup: Kephri: ranged (Twisted bow on boss, chinchompas for scarab swarms); BiS tri-brid.
456.strategy: Path 4 + all Kephri invocations (incl. Medic) make scarab swarms and healing harder. Chin the swarms, kill bodyguards during shield phases, dodge the 3x3 aerial bombs and dung strikes. Survive yourself only.

457.setup: Kephri: high ranged DPS (Twisted bow), chinchompas for the Medic scarab swarms; BiS ranged.
457.strategy: With Medic active she summons swarms that heal her; after the first down keep DPS high and clear the healing scarabs fast so she never recovers past 25%. Chin the swarms immediately and burst her between shield phases.

458.setup: High special-attack DPS (e.g. specs for staggers) plus strong main DPS; brews/restores
458.strategy: In phase two, limit yourself to at most two staggers of the boss (manage your special-attack timing) while still pushing enough damage to finish the phase, then survive to the end.

459.setup: Top-tier P3 DPS (Tbow/Bowfa or Shadow) with full prayer support; brews/restores
459.strategy: Enable Aerial Assault, Stay Vigilant and Insanity, then DPS Elidinis' Warden hard in P3 while dodging the harder lightning/skull/demi-god mechanics these invocations add, surviving to the kill.

460.strategy: All Wardens invocations (incl. Insanity) raise p3 slam speed and damage. Handle obelisks in p2, pre-pray and reposition for p3 slams/skulls. You only need to survive yourself, not perfect. Most accessible of the Wardens CAs here (13.4%).

Note spikes heal the boss if stepped on, so position carefully.

All three must register during the same kill, easiest done on the prayer-shield phase.

Only the unavoidable melee strikes in melee form (which hit through prayer) do not count. Prayer-flick perfectly and pre-walk away from spikes/clouds.

Hardest combo - bank the spike phase for the kill and never let a single avoidable hit land while walking-only.
