569.strategy: Just accumulate 10 kills; no special restriction. Learn the prayer-swap pattern for the feather projectiles to reduce food usage.

570.setup: Crush melee with Piety or earth magic; 25 total kills. Group scaling helps grind quickly.
570.strategy: Pure grind to 25 kills. Will likely complete naturally while doing the harder mechanical/speed tasks.

571.setup: High-DPS crush melee (DHL/scythe) plus reliable overhead prayer swapping; consider a small organised team for cleaner mechanics.
571.strategy: Step off glowing lightning symbols within 4 ticks, move away from the tail before it slams (shockwave 20-25), and swap overhead prayer to match each feather colour (red=melee, green=ranged, blue=magic). Chip/poison/unavoidable hits are allowed; only avoidable damage fails it.

572.setup: Crush melee or earth magic; coordinate prayers to charge pillars. Team makes charging the three pillars (Protect Magic/Melee/Missiles) easier.
572.strategy: Charge all three pillars by maintaining balanced protection prayers to light the braziers (30% damage buff), then time the kill to land during that buffed window.

573.stats: 80+ Attack, 90+ Strength, 70+ Prayer, 70+ Ranged (for DHCB option)
573.setup: Dragon hunter lance is the strongest option (crush style hits the 0 crush defence). Dragon hunter wand/crossbow also qualify but are far weaker here.
573.strategy: Use the Dragon hunter lance for the whole kill (it gets the dragonbane bonus and exploits 0 crush defence). Note the lance is melee-capped during the tail-break phase, so expect a slower kill; bring extra supplies.
573.items: Dragon hunter lance; Dragon hunter crossbow; Dragon hunter wand

574.setup: Best-tier earth spell you can cast (Earth surge ideally) on standard spellbook; Tome of earth, occult, magic gear. The boss has 60% earth weakness.
574.strategy: Cast only earth spells the entire kill; the Hueycoatl's elemental weakness is Earth (60%), so earth magic is naturally strong here. Tome of earth boosts damage and saves runes.
574.items: Earth wave; Earth surge; Earth blast; Earth bolt; Earth strike; Mud battlestaff; Earth runes; Tome of earth

575.stats: 80+ Attack, 90+ Strength, 70+ Prayer; 60+ Ranged/Defence to wear the hide armour
575.setup: Equip any two Hueycoatl hide pieces (coif, body, chaps, vambraces) and otherwise fight normally (crush melee or earth magic).
575.strategy: The armour drops from this very boss, so do early kills first, then re-enter wearing two pieces. They are Ranged armour, so pairing with a ranged setup avoids a stats penalty, but any style works as long as two pieces are worn.
575.items: Hueycoatl hide coif; Hueycoatl hide body; Hueycoatl hide chaps; Hueycoatl hide vambraces

576.setup: Max DPS crush melee (scythe/DHL) and/or earth magic in a larger team to burn the 2500 HP fast.
576.strategy: Bring as many players as possible (up to 20) so the combined DPS clears the body, head and tail well under 2:30. Charge pillars early for the 30% buff to speed the kill.

577.setup: Strong gear on all 5 players: scythe/DHL crush, earth surge, Thralls. Coordinate pillar charging for the damage buff.
577.strategy: With only 5 players you need high per-player DPS and clean mechanics; charge all three pillars for the 30% buff and minimise downtime on the tail-break phase to hit 2:30.

578.setup: Near-max gear on all 3 players: scythe/DHL, occult + earth surge, Thralls, best prayers. Tight pillar/prayer coordination essential.
578.strategy: With 3 or fewer players, maximise every player's DPS (best-in-slot crush melee plus earth magic), keep the 30% brazier buff active, and execute mechanics flawlessly to avoid wasted ticks; this is the hardest Hueycoatl speed task.

579.setup: Melee, crush weapon (Inquisitor's mace / Abyssal bludgeon / Dragon mace), Protect from Magic
579.strategy: Pray Protect from Magic for the ice spikes and reactivate it after Icicle Crash strips prayer; melee her down for a single kill.

580.setup: Melee crush weapon (Inquisitor's mace / dual macuahuitl / Abyssal bludgeon), Protect from Magic, prayer pots + brews
580.strategy: 20 kills is just sustained KC; bring prayer potions and food, and destroy unstable ice blocks each time to avoid the boss healing.

581.setup: High-DPS melee (Scythe of Vitur or dual macuahuitl + Blood moon), Divine super combat, spec weapon (Burning claws/DDS), Thralls
581.strategy: Open with a special attack, use a damage thrall, and keep continuous DPS; Scythe is ideal because it cleaves the ice blocks while hitting the boss so you never stall.

582.stats: 90+ Attack/Strength, 70+ Defence, 44+ Prayer, max melee gear
582.setup: BiS melee: Scythe of Vitur, Inquisitor's/Torva, Ultor ring, Amulet of rancour, Divine super combat, Burning claws spec, damage Thralls
582.strategy: Maximise burst: pre-pot, open with Burning claws spec, spam damage thralls and use Scythe so its cleave clears ice blocks without breaking your attack rhythm; needs near-perfect DPS.

583.setup: Melee crush weapon with Protect from Magic; high DPS to shorten the fight reduces mistakes
583.strategy: Keep Protect from Magic on for the ice spikes, immediately re-flick it after each Icicle Crash (which strips prayers), and step off icy pool tiles by dragging her to fresh tiles so the typeless tick damage never lands.

584.setup: Glacial temotli (crush, dropped by Amoxliatl), best crush armour (Inquisitor's/Torva), Protect from Magic
584.strategy: Equip the glacial temotli (a boss drop) and use no other weapon or spec weapon; it still destroys the unstable ice blocks like any melee weapon, so just pray Protect from Magic and grind it out.

585.setup: Melee crush gear, NO protection prayers used; rely on high Defence and food instead of prayer
585.strategy: Do not activate any prayers at all, and avoid the Icicle Crash (which drains prayer) by killing fast or out-positioning it; tank the ice spikes with food/Defence since flicking Protect from Magic would consume prayer points.

586.setup: Melee crush gear, prayer potions, Saradomin brews/food, Divine super combat for the full run
586.strategy: Bring enough supplies for 10 consecutive kills in one trip; do not exit to bank or restock, and keep destroying ice blocks so the boss doesn't heal and waste your supplies.

587.setup: Melee crush weapon (Scythe of Vitur ideal for cleaving blocks), Protect from Magic
587.strategy: Destroy every unstable ice block within the 15-tick window before it explodes; a single melee hit instantly breaks a block, and the Scythe can clear multiple while still hitting the boss so none are left to shatter and heal her.

588.stats: 75+ Magic, 70+ Ranged, 43+ Prayer (70+ for Piety), 70+ Defence
588.setup: Twinflame staff (auto-swaps water/fire) or a ranged setup; Protect from Melee for stomps. Bring food/restores even though goal is no damage.
588.strategy: Run away from the stomp 3x5 zone (or use Protect from Melee), stand off elementals so their 8-10 self-destruct can't reach you, kill icicle/fire spawns or move off them, and always step to the single safe converging tile during the phase-2 elemental blast pulses.

589.setup: Twinflame staff for magic, or melee (Dragon scimitar/Abyssal tentacle) plus a thrown/ranged switch; Protect from Melee for stomps.
589.strategy: Plain kill count - just complete 10 kills; duo with a partner of combat 90+ to speed it up, exploit each titan's opposing-element weakness.

590.setup: Twinflame staff or mixed melee/ranged with gear switches; Protect from Melee.
590.strategy: Accumulates with the 10-kill task - just reach 25 total kills; consistent duo runs make this fast.

591.setup: Single-style burst setup (Twinflame staff or strong melee) to focus one target; Protect from Melee.
591.strategy: Attack only one titan the entire time. Note that killing one titan first lets the survivor's stomp deal ~10 damage through prayer, so be ready to tank/heal once the first dies.

592.setup: High DPS: Twinflame staff hitting each titan's weak element, or Toxic blowpipe / Bow of faerdhinen / Scythe of vitur; bring Saradomin brews/restores and damage-boost prayers.
592.strategy: Best done as a duo for combined DPS. Hit Eldric (ice) with fire and Branda (fire) with water - the Twinflame staff auto-swaps - and ignore optional mechanics, prioritising raw damage to beat the 1:30 timer.

593.setup: Sustainable supplies for 15 back-to-back kills (food, prayer restores) and a reliable Twinflame/melee setup; Protect from Melee.
593.strategy: Stay in the same instance for all 15 kills - do not leave or let a partner leave. Either solo it or keep a fixed duo; bring enough supplies to avoid banking mid-streak.

594.setup: Single-target damage on the titans only; bring food to absorb the 8-10 elemental self-destructs since you can't kill them.
594.strategy: Never click an elemental. Position so the elementals' self-destruct (8-10 in a 3x3) misses you, or simply eat through the chip damage while focusing the titans.

595.stats: 75+ Magic (Fire Blast / Water Blast or higher), 43+ Prayer
595.setup: Twinflame staff is ideal - its passive auto-swaps between fire and water spells so one weapon douses fires (Water Blast+) and melts icicles (Fire Blast+); otherwise carry both spell runes/tomes.
595.strategy: Douse fires with Water Blast or greater and melt icicles with Fire Blast or greater (each clears adjacent obstacles too); keep up with spawns so none vanish on their own - the Twinflame staff's auto-element-swap makes this far easier.
595.items: Twinflame staff; Tome of fire; Tome of water

596.stats: 75+ Magic, 70+ Defence, high HP for tanking
596.setup: Tank gear with high Defence; do NOT use protection or overhead prayers. Optionally Prayer-regen items, but you must avoid activating any prayer that drains points.
596.strategy: Don't activate Protect from Melee or any other prayer - instead avoid the stomp 3x5 zone by moving out of melee range, dodge elemental explosions and blast pulses manually, and tank/eat the rest so prayer never ticks down.

597.setup: Standard kill setup; you simply refrain from finishing elementals so the titans' attacks destroy them.
597.strategy: The titans damage and can kill the opposing elementals - leave the elementals alone (don't last-hit them) and let the titans' attacks rack up 10 elemental kills over one or more runs.

598.setup: High burst DPS hitting both weaknesses (Twinflame staff, or strong ranged/melee) so you can finish both at once; ideally a duo to split the two titans.
598.strategy: Bring both titans to near-death, then when they begin charging their arena-wide area attack, deal the killing blows to both within that same charge window so they die during one charging phase.

599.stats: 82+ Magic, 80+ Attack/Strength, 75+ Defence, 70+ Prayer (77+ for Augury)
599.setup: Magic (Tumeken's Shadow + Ancestral + Elidinis' Ward (f), Magus ring, Occult) or Melee (Emberlight/Scythe + Avernic + Torva/Oathplate, Ultor); bring a Purging staff for void flares and magic for the Judge.
599.strategy: A single kill. Learn the phase transitions at 66.6% and 33.3% HP; magic is the most forgiving learning style. Kill the Judge with magic (always prays melee+ranged first) and destroy void flares so they don't heal Yama. Requires A Kingdom Divided completed.

600.setup: Same as the single kill - efficient magic (Tumeken's Shadow) or melee (Emberlight/Scythe) setup with Purging staff for flares; bring magic for the Judge.
600.strategy: Defeat Yama 50 times - grind achievement. Settle into a consistent rotation (kill the Judge with magic, clear void flares each phase) to make repeat kills reliable rather than fast.

601.setup: High-DPS magic (Tumeken's Shadow + max mage) or melee (Emberlight/Scythe of Vitur + max melee, Ultor); Elder Maul + Soulflame Horn or Purging staff special for DPS uptime.
601.strategy: Need a sub-3:38 average across four consecutive kills. Use a damage-boosting Mark of Darkness + Dark Demonbane rotation, kill the Judge fast with magic, and don't waste ticks on void flares. Achievable with solid mechanics and near-max gear.

602.setup: Best-in-slot magic (Tumeken's Shadow) and/or melee (Scythe of Vitur/Emberlight, Ultor, Avernic); Elder Maul + Soulflame Horn for defence reduction, Purging staff special for flare speedups.
602.strategy: Sub-3:00 average over four kills demands tight DPS. Pre-cast Mark of Darkness/Dark Demonbane, use Purging staff finishes on void flares for attack-cooldown reduction, and kill the Judge instantly with magic each intermission.

603.setup: Max-gear DPS: Tumeken's Shadow magic and Scythe of Vitur/Emberlight melee; Elder Maul + Soulflame Horn, Blue Moon spear special for the Robospear unfreeze, Purging staff for flares.
603.strategy: Sub-2:34 average is a high-end speedrun. Requires tick-perfect Phase 3 play (Donofly cycle or Robospear blue-moon-spear unfreeze for one-tick speedups), instant Judge kills, and near-zero wasted ticks.

604.setup: Tanky max setup so you can survive on plain pizza only - high Defence armour (Torva/Oathplate or Ancestral), Avernic/Elidinis' Ward, no potions allowed so rely on high base stats.
604.strategy: Bring only plain pizza for food (no other food, no potions of any kind). Play defensively - flick prayers correctly, dodge shadows/fire, and clear void flares so Yama can't out-heal your reduced healing throughput.

605.setup: Any viable style; magic (Tumeken's Shadow) is forgiving for movement. Keep Mark of Darkness up for the attack-speed bonus when positioned on the shadow crash.
605.strategy: Shadow crash drops three fireballs in a line; the side fireballs spawn shadows that converge toward the centre fireball (bowtie). Stay within one tile of the centre and move perpendicular to dodge the converging shadows; also skip over shadow waves from the corners. The Donofly tile cycle auto-dodges these.

606.setup: Purging staff (its special is ideal for finishing void flares) plus a main DPS weapon; spec weapons / spec energy management needed since every flare must die to a special attack.
606.strategy: Fire streaks travel horizontally from arena edges - run through without pausing to avoid damage, and stand on the correct glyph. Every void flare must be killed with a special attack (Purging staff special is best; manage spec energy across all flares).

607.setup: Bring magic for the Judge (it always prays melee+ranged first, then reactively against your last style) plus your main DPS style; have the Judge-killing weapon ready before each exile.
607.strategy: Each time you are exiled (at 66.6% and 33.3% HP) you cross the lava platforms to the Judge. Attack it only with magic so you never hit a protected style, and take no more than one hit per exile - move carefully across platforms and pray correctly.

608.setup: Magic main style (Tumeken's Shadow) for forgiving movement, Purging staff for special-attack flare kills, and magic for the Judge - one setup that satisfies all three sub-tasks at once.
608.strategy: Stack all three Master mechanicals plus take no other avoidable damage. Use the Donofly cycle to auto-dodge shadows/crashes, run through fire without stopping, finish every void flare with a special (Purging staff), hit the Judge only with magic, and avoid any other avoidable hit.

609.setup: Best-in-slot for your style (Tumeken's Shadow magic or Scythe/Emberlight melee) with strong defensive gear; pick the contract whose modifier least disrupts your playstyle.
609.strategy: Take on a non-standard contract - e.g. Bloodied Blows (3000 HP, damage thresholds), Forfeit Breath (run energy drains to 0 = death), Glyphic Attenuation (weak glyphs), Sensory Clouding (relentless melee, prayers don't block) or Divine Severance (prayers disable after 2 ticks, flares one-shot). Choose the contract that punishes your weakest mechanic least and play conservatively.

610.setup: Demonbane/ranged switch (Scorching bow + holy water, or Emberlight melee), Eye of Ayak charged, blessed dragonhide or melee armour, protection prayers + Rigour/Piety
610.strategy: Plain kill count at delve level 3: just survive and out-DPS the boss while clearing larvae before they reach it; pray against the green (ranged) and blue (magic) orbs and re-flick after rock throws disable prayer.

611.setup: Best-in-slot demonbane (Scorching bow + holy water, Emberlight, Burning claws), Eye of Ayak charged with demon tears, blessed d'hide/Masori or Torva, Rigour/Piety + protection prayers, strong food/Saradomin brews
611.strategy: Must streak all the way to delve 8 in one continuous run (HP scales to 625 and larvae become single giant grubs with tripled absorption penalty) - bring enough supplies for the long fight and keep the boss from absorbing grubs.

612.setup: Top-tier demonbane setup (Scorching bow + holy water, Emberlight, Eye of Ayak), Masori/Torva BiS, full prayer switching, large supply stack (multiple brews + restores)
612.strategy: An extreme endurance run: deep delves keep delve-8 HP (625) but stack enhanced mechanics each level, so manage prayer/supplies tightly and execute every mechanic cleanly across 16 consecutive delves.

613.setup: Demonbane melee weapon (Emberlight/Burning claws) plus holy water for the punish, melee armour, protection prayers
613.strategy: Bring the boss low on HP, then position correctly into the Melee charge phase and use the melee punish to finish it - recurrent melee-punish damage now triggers the holy water effect if it secures the kill.

614.setup: Highest-DPS demonbane setup (Scorching bow + holy water / Emberlight), Eye of Ayak, BiS offensive gear and prayers to maximise burst damage
614.strategy: Streak to level 8+ then unload maximum DPS so the boss dies during/before completing its first burrow transition - requires it to be near death entering the level and a clean larvae/special clear.

615.setup: Holy water (one-shots ranged larvae) + Eye of Ayak (one-shots magic larvae) + demonbane spell/weapon for fast larva clears, BiS DPS gear
615.strategy: Prioritise killing every grub/larva before it reaches the boss across all 8 delves - use holy water on ranged larvae and a charged Eye of Ayak on magic larvae to one-shot them; remember level 8 spawns a single giant grub with tripled absorption penalty.

616.setup: BiS demonbane DPS (Scorching bow + holy water, Eye of Ayak), full prayer switching, Earthen shield ability to clear acid blood from your path
616.strategy: Flawless 8-delve run: perfectly pray/avoid every orb, melee and rock-throw, kill all grubs before absorption, and stay off acid splats (use the Earthen shield to clear acid blood) - the rock-throw prayer-disable is the main threat to the no-damage requirement.

617.stats: 90+ Ranged, 85+ Magic, 78+ Defence, 92+ Hitpoints, 77+ Prayer (high Prayer level helps the pool last)
617.setup: Prayer-efficient gear (max Prayer bonus), demonbane DPS to end fights fast, lazy/precise prayer flicking to conserve points
617.strategy: Bring no prayer restores and ration your starting prayer pool across all 8 delves - flick protection prayers only on incoming orbs/attacks and lean on gear prayer bonus; high Prayer level and fast kills are essential.

618.setup: One-handed demonbane melee (Emberlight or Burning claws) with a defensive shield/holy water offhand, Torva/melee BiS, Piety + protection prayers
618.strategy: Use only one-handed melee weapons to deal all boss damage across 8 delves - Emberlight or Burning claws (demonbane, one-handed) are ideal; you must still kill larvae (use holy water/Eye effects), and the all-melee restriction makes positioning against orbs/rock-throws harder.
618.items: The Final Dawn (quest complete); Emberlight; Arclight; Burning claws; Bone dagger; Dragon dagger; Ghrazi rapier; Blade of saeldor; Osmumten's fang

619.setup: Non-demonbane BiS (e.g. Twisted bow/Masori ranged, Tumeken's shadow magic, or Scythe/Ghrazi melee) - no demonbane weapons or holy water allowed; full prayer switching
619.strategy: Clear all 8 delves without ever equipping a demonbane weapon, which removes the larva one-shot/attack-delay bonuses - bring strong non-demonbane DPS and expect slower larva clears, so manage grub absorption and the longer fight carefully.

620.stats: 90+ Attack/Strength or Ranged, 80+ Defence, 92+ Hitpoints, 77+ Prayer
620.setup: One-handed demonbane weapon (Emberlight/Burning claws or a one-handed ranged option) plus a permanent shield (Avernic defender, Twisted buckler, or Spectral spirit shield), defensive armour
620.strategy: Keep a shield equipped the entire 8-delve run, so you are locked into one-handed weapons - pair a one-handed demonbane weapon with Avernic defender (melee) or a buckler for ranged; the constant shield helps survivability but limits DPS so plan a longer fight.
620.items: The Final Dawn (quest complete); Avernic defender; Dragon defender; Twisted buckler; Spectral spirit shield; Crystal shield

621.setup: Maximum burst DPS demonbane (Scorching bow + holy water / Eye of Ayak), full offensive prayers (Rigour/Piety), DPS-overload boosts
621.strategy: Level 1 has the lowest HP (525) - bring max-DPS demonbane gear, use offensive prayers and boosts, and burst the boss down within 30 seconds; a special-attack opener helps secure the time.

622.setup: High-DPS demonbane setup (Scorching bow + holy water, Eye of Ayak), BiS offensive gear and prayers, minimal downtime
622.strategy: Maintain high uptime DPS across all 8 delves and clear larvae/specials quickly to keep the run under 10:00 - efficient one-shotting of larvae (holy water/Eye of Ayak) saves the most time.

623.setup: Absolute BiS demonbane DPS (Scorching bow + holy water, Eye of Ayak, Masori/Torva), perfect prayer/gear switching, overload-tier boosts
623.strategy: A near-perfect speed run: maximise DPS uptime, never miss a larva one-shot, and execute every special/burrow phase optimally to break 7:15 across all 8 delves - leaves almost no margin for mistakes.

624.setup: Standard Doom DPS setup with the Earthen shield ability available to clear acid blood/splats
624.strategy: During a Volatile Earth special, interact with the volatile earth objects and use the Earthen shield (which removes acid blood in its path) to clear 8 or more acid splats in that one special - let several splats accumulate, then clear them in a single sweep to hit the count.

625.stats: 65+ Attack, 65+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer, 51 Slayer, 50+ HP
625.setup: Melee. Tortugan shield (back, mandatory), Ghrazi rapier/Scythe + Avernic defender, best melee body/legs, Slayer helm, Protect from Melee + Piety.
625.strategy: Plain kill count x25. Equip the Tortugan shield and pray Protect from Melee to fully negate melee damage; move one tile each attack to avoid whirlwind stacks and corrosive spit.

626.stats: 65+ Attack, 65+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer, 51 Slayer, 60+ HP
626.setup: Melee. Tortugan shield + best-in-slot melee (rapier/scythe, Avernic defender, Torva/Oathplate), Protect from Melee + Piety, prayer potions.
626.strategy: Plain kill count x50. Same approach as the 25-kill task; bring extra prayer potions and keep moving to dodge whirlwinds across the longer grind.

627.stats: 70+ Attack, 70+ Strength, 75+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 60+ HP, 51 Slayer
627.setup: Melee with Tortugan shield (back) + heavy 40kg+ gear, Avernic defender, Protect from Melee + Piety. High DPS weapon (Scythe of vitur/Ghrazi rapier) to shorten the kill.
627.strategy: No-damage kill. Equip Tortugan shield + Protect from Melee to fully negate melee; wear 40kg+ gear so knockback never triggers; move one tile every attack to avoid whirlwind stacks and step off corrosive spit before it lands.

628.stats: 75+ Attack, 80+ Strength, 75+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 70+ HP, 51 Slayer
628.setup: Max melee DPS: Scythe of vitur, Tortugan shield + Avernic defender, Torva, Ferocious gloves, Ultor ring; Piety; special-attack weapon and divine/super combat potions.
628.strategy: Speedrun x10 in 12:30. Maximise DPS (scythe + specs, Piety) and never leave the cave; use the knockback-counter stun for free hits and keep moving to avoid whirlwinds that would slow kills.

629.stats: 70+ Attack, 75+ Strength, 75+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 70+ HP, 51 Slayer
629.setup: Melee with Tortugan shield + Protect from Melee + Piety; bring a full inventory of prayer potions, food, and a high-DPS weapon to sustain 25 kills in one trip.
629.strategy: Stamina run of 25 kills without leaving. Prioritise inventory of prayer potions/food over extras; pray Protect from Melee with the shield to negate melee and conserve supplies across the whole trip.

630.stats: 65+ Attack, 65+ Strength, 70+ Defence, 43+ Prayer, 51 Slayer, 50+ HP
630.setup: Standard melee setup with Tortugan shield; Protect from Melee + Piety. Nothing special needed beyond reacting to the corrosive spit.
630.strategy: Let the gryphon land a corrosive spit, then clear it (step one tile away or briefly unequip to interrupt the corrosion) before finishing the kill - the achievement requires the spit be removed from your armour first.

631.stats: 70+ Attack, 75+ Strength, 75+ Defence, 70+ Prayer, 80+ HP, 51 Slayer
631.setup: Lightweight melee: Tortugan shield (back) plus low-weight pieces to stay under 40kg; Protect from Melee + Piety, plenty of food/prayer potions to absorb knockback.
631.strategy: Under-40kg means knockback (up to 30 unavoidable damage) WILL trigger - bring extra food and brews. Still equip the Tortugan shield + Protect from Melee to negate melee, and keep moving to dodge whirlwinds and corrosive spit across all 5 kills in one trip.

632.stats: Any combat level; 40+ in any combat style and 43+ Prayer helpful
632.setup: Any weapon you can wield; Protect from Melee prayer if available. Earth spells (e.g. Earth Bolt/Wave) hit 25% harder if going magic.
632.strategy: Plain kill count. Fight Brutus in the instanced arena after starting/finishing The Ides of Milk; pray Protect from Melee or dodge the two telegraphed specials (up to 19 damage) and chip down his 58 HP.

633.stats: 45+ in any combat style, 43+ Prayer recommended
633.setup: Any decent F2P weapon (e.g. rune scimitar) or earth spells (25% weakness); Protect from Melee prayer.
633.strategy: Same fight repeated 10 times. Use Protect from Melee to negate standard hits and step away from telegraphed specials each kill to stay efficient.

634.stats: 94+ Magic for high earth spells, or maxed melee/Strength; high combat stats for max burst
634.setup: Exploit the 25% earth weakness with a high-hitting earth spell (e.g. Earth Wave/Surge or Bind-tier nuke) plus best magic gear, OR a max-hit melee spec weapon. Stack max DPS so 58 HP dies within ~3 ticks.
634.strategy: Under 2 seconds is ~3 game ticks. Open with the strongest possible burst on full HP: earth magic gains the 25% weakness bonus, making a single big earth hit (or hit + autos) the most reliable way to delete his 58 HP instantly.
634.items: Earth surge runes (Earth/Smoke/maxed earth spell); Best-in-slot DPS weapon (e.g. high-tier magic or melee)

635.stats: 50+ Attack/Strength to punch through 58 HP unarmed; higher melee stats speed it up
635.setup: Completely unequipped (no weapon or armour) and use no consumables. Punch Brutus to death using unarmed attacks.
635.strategy: You cannot equip anything or eat/drink, so survival relies entirely on dodging. Move out of the two telegraphed specials (which can hit ~19) and keep distance-managing while you punch him down; bring high HP and good melee levels so the unarmed kill is fast.

636.stats: 70+ in your combat style for a quick kill; 43+ Prayer for Protect from Melee backup
636.setup: Ranged or magic for safe damage from distance, or melee with careful movement. Protect from Melee can negate standard hits; earth magic adds 25% damage.
636.strategy: Brutus cannot flinch but his attacks are telegraphed. Dodge both special attacks by moving the few ticks they give you, and avoid all standard melee hits via safespotting/distance or Protect from Melee, so no attack ever lands while you kill his 58 HP.
